lead_en: Donald Trump says he believes the United States will “get Greenland” and frames control of the island as necessary for the security of the free world.
justification: The article is primarily about Greenland’s strategic importance and Trump’s foreign-policy rhetoric about U.S. control, which fits security/foreign policy most closely.

headline_no: T-banen stenger i sentrum på kveldstid
headline_en: The metro is closing in the city centre in the evenings
lead_en: Evening metro traffic through central Oslo will be disrupted until 13 March because Sporveien is installing a new signalling and safety system.
justification: The article is a local transport and infrastructure notice about metro closures, which does not fit the main political issue categories.

section_no: Nyheter | Israel Og Hamas I Krig
headline_no: Unrwa-forbudet trer i kraft – følgene kan bli dramatiske
headline_en: The UNRWA ban takes effect – the consequences could be dramatic
lead_en: Israel’s ban on UNRWA is about to take effect, raising fears that millions of Palestinians could lose access to schooling, health care, and humanitarian aid.
justification: The article is primarily about Gaza/Palestine and the consequences of Israel’s ban on UNRWA, while also highlighting Labour leaders Støre and Eide criticizing the move.

headline_no: Vis frem meldingene mellom Tangen og Musk!
headline_en: Show the messages between Tangen and Musk!
lead_en: Norges Bank and the Finance Ministry have refused access to text messages between Oil Fund chief Nicolai Tangen and Elon Musk, and E24 has appealed the case to the Parliamentary Ombud. The article argues that the messages concern public duties and should be disclosed under Norway’s transparency laws.
justification: The article is primarily about transparency, public access to documents, and trust in public administration, which fits governance rather than a substantive policy area.

headline_no: Gjør seg høy og mørk i innvandringsdebatten
headline_en: Acting tough in the immigration debate
lead_en: Germany’s opposition leader Friedrich Merz is moving closer to the far right on immigration after deadly attacks involving asylum seekers. The article says he is not alone, while business leaders warn against the economic costs of anti-immigration politics.
justification: The article’s main focus is immigration, asylum, deportations, and crime linked to the German election campaign, with a secondary emphasis on party competition and coalition politics.

headline_no: Fagarbeidere kan kjøpe 0 prosent av boligene i Oslo
headline_en: Skilled workers can buy 0 percent of the homes in Oslo
lead_en: New figures from LO show that workers on typical skilled-trade wages are effectively shut out of the housing market in Oslo and face severe barriers in other Norwegian cities. The article highlights how ordinary full-time workers struggle to buy a home even with stable employment.
justification: The article is primarily about housing affordability and economic hardship for ordinary workers, which fits the cost-of-living/economy category.

headline_no: Mehl vil la politiet ransake folk som ikke er mistenkte
headline_en: Mehl wants to let the police search people who are not suspects
lead_en: Justice Minister Emilie Enger Mehl wants Norway to introduce police search zones modeled on Sweden and Denmark, allowing people to be stopped and searched without individualized suspicion. The article highlights criticism from legal experts and notes that police authorities themselves say such a measure is not currently needed.
justification: The article is primarily about crime prevention and police powers in response to violence and gang crime, with a secondary governance dimension concerning legal safeguards and ministerial judgment.

headline_no: – Norge har mistet kontrollen
headline_en: “Norway has lost control”
lead_en: Centre Party leader Trygve Slagsvold Vedum says Norway has lost control over electricity policy and wants to push the limits of national control over foreign power cables. The article centers on the government dispute over EU energy directives and efforts to secure lower, more stable power prices.
justification: The article is mainly about electricity prices, energy market control, and foreign power cables, with a secondary governance dimension because it is tied to coalition conflict inside the government.

headline_no: Kjetil B. Alstadheim: Senterpartiet er på glid. Det som var uaktuelt for partiet i forrige uke, er nå høyst aktuelt.
headline_en: Kjetil B. Alstadheim: The Centre Party is shifting. What was unacceptable for the party last week is now highly relevant.
lead_en: The article argues that the Centre Party is softening its position in the dispute over the EU clean energy package and is now seeking a political win rather than issuing an ultimatum. It frames the conflict as negotiations over power cables, electricity control, and Norway’s relationship with Europe.
justification: The article is primarily about coalition bargaining, party maneuvering, and government conflict over the EU energy package, with electricity policy as the secondary substantive issue.

headline_no: Aker Solutions tildelt milliardkontrakt
headline_en: Aker Solutions awarded billion-kroner contract
lead_en: Aker Solutions and SLB Capturi have been awarded a major contract to build a carbon capture facility at the Klemetsrud waste-to-energy plant in Oslo. The restarted project is framed as a key climate and industrial initiative backed by both the state and Oslo municipality.
justification: The article is primarily about carbon capture, emissions cuts, and climate policy, with a secondary economic dimension due to the large industrial contract and public funding.

headline_no: Presidenten er misfornøyd. Politiet har fått kvoter for hvor mange de skal arrestere daglig.
headline_en: The president is dissatisfied. The police have been given quotas for how many they must arrest each day.
lead_en: The Trump administration has ordered immigration authorities to sharply increase daily arrests of undocumented migrants, with local offices given fixed quotas. The article also describes broader aggressive measures to enforce Trump’s immigration and executive agenda.
justification: The article is mainly about immigration enforcement, deportations, and treatment of undocumented migrants, with a secondary focus on executive power and rule-of-law concerns.

headline_no: Trump får det han vil ha. Det er likevel ikke nok for ham.
headline_en: Trump gets what he wants. Even so, it is not enough for him.
lead_en: Danes feel doubly threatened: by Donald Trump’s pressure over Greenland and by Greenland’s growing push for independence from Denmark. The article argues that the U.S. can secure greater strategic access without force, yet Trump appears to want more.
justification: The article is primarily about geopolitics, territorial pressure, Arctic strategy, and security relations involving the U.S., Denmark, and Greenland.

headline_no: Grønlandsk politiker: -Trump skaper frykt
headline_en: Greenlandic politician: “Trump is creating fear”
lead_en: A Greenlandic member of the Danish parliament says Donald Trump’s rhetoric is making people in Greenland fearful and worried about their children’s future. She rejects the idea of Greenland becoming American and says the island’s future must be decided democratically by Greenlanders themselves.
justification: The article is primarily about security, Arctic geopolitics, and U.S. pressure over Greenland, with a secondary governance dimension concerning self-determination and electoral influence; it also involves Norway through a meeting in Tromsø with Norwegian parliamentarians.

headline_no: Framaga til Fafo: Trump har en ny forkortelse
headline_en: From MAGA to FAFO: Trump has a new abbreviation
lead_en: During a standoff with Colombia over deportation flights, Donald Trump posted an image of himself with the slang acronym “FAFO.” The article explains the threat-laden message and links it to Trump’s broader push for mass deportations and pressure on foreign governments to accept returnees.
justification: The article is mainly about deportations and immigration enforcement, with a secondary foreign-policy dimension because it centers on U.S. pressure on Colombia over accepting returned migrants.

headline_no: Jan Bøhler forlater Sp og stiller på protestliste
headline_en: Jan Bøhler leaves the Centre Party and runs on a protest list
lead_en: Former MP Jan Bøhler is leaving the Centre Party to run for parliament on the single-issue list Redd Ullevål sykehus. The article frames this as a protest candidacy aimed at influencing the future of hospital policy in Oslo.
justification: The article is mainly about electoral strategy, party switching, and parliamentary positioning around a protest list, with hospital policy as the secondary issue.

headline_no: Fire dømt etter hotellkonkurs
headline_en: Four convicted after hotel bankruptcy
lead_en: Four people involved in running the hotel company Maribel have been sentenced to prison for extensive financial crime after the company’s 2020 bankruptcy. The case centers on fictitious agreements, tax fraud, breach of trust, and using company funds for private purposes.
justification: The article is primarily about corporate financial crime and criminal convictions rather than an election-related policy domain, so it fits Other, with a secondary governance angle due to legal enforcement and accountability.

headline_no: 23 pågrepet i ukrainskledet kriminelt nettverk De pågrepne skal ha misbrukt flyktningstatusen fra Ukraina, mener Europol.
headline_en: 23 arrested in a Ukrainian-led criminal network. The suspects are believed to have abused refugee status from Ukraine, according to Europol.
lead_en: Twenty-three people across several European countries have been arrested in a major police operation against a criminal network led by two Ukrainian brothers. According to Europol, the group exploited the temporary protection status granted to Ukrainian refugees in the EU.
justification: The article is primarily about abuse of refugee protection status and cross-border crime linked to migration, which fits immigration/integration/crime.

headline_no: Falt på glatta og brakk håndleddet. Nå får kommunen gjennomgå.
headline_en: Slipped on the ice and broke her wrist. Now the municipality is being criticized.
lead_en: Author Erika Fatland broke her wrist after slipping on ice along the Akerselva in Oslo and is now criticizing the municipality for poor gritting. The article links her case to wider concerns about unsafe winter streets, sick leave, and municipal maintenance.
justification: The article is primarily about injury, public safety, and strain on health services from icy streets, with a secondary governance angle because it criticizes municipal service performance.

headline_no: 80 år siden Auschwitz. Men hva har verden egentlig lært?
headline_en: 80 years since Auschwitz. But what has the world really learned?
lead_en: World leaders once again gathered to commemorate the liberation of Auschwitz, but the wars in Ukraine and Gaza overshadow the anniversary. The article reflects on Holocaust memory, antisemitism, and the tension between remembrance and ongoing conflict.
justification: The article is primarily about international conflict, war, and geopolitical tensions surrounding the Auschwitz commemoration, with Gaza as a clear secondary theme.

headline_no: Fuglefjær og blod funnet i flymotorene
headline_en: Bird feathers and blood found in the aircraft engines
lead_en: Bird feathers and blood were found in both engines of the plane that crash-landed in South Korea on 29 December. Investigators are examining possible bird strike involvement, but the cause of the crash remains unclear.
justification: The article is about an aviation accident investigation in South Korea and does not fit the main election-related policy categories, so it belongs in Other.

headline_no: Eu-landene sier ja til å forlenge sanksjoner mot Russland
headline_en: EU countries agree to extend sanctions against Russia
lead_en: EU foreign ministers unanimously agreed to extend sanctions on Russia for another six months despite earlier resistance from Hungary. The decision keeps Russian assets frozen and preserves economic pressure linked to the war in Ukraine.
justification: The article is primarily about foreign policy and the sanctions regime tied to Russia’s war in Ukraine, with a secondary economic dimension because it concerns energy flows, trade restrictions, and frozen assets.

headline_no: Nei, man bør ikke gi skattefradrag formigrantoverføringer
headline_en: No, one should not give tax deductions for migrant remittances
lead_en: The article argues against introducing tax deductions for money transfers from immigrants in Norway to family abroad. It says such a measure would reduce Norwegian tax revenues, be unfairly targeted, and do little to address poverty in Norway.
justification: The article’s core focus is whether the tax system should subsidize remittances, making tax policy and redistribution the primary issue.

headline_no: Inflasjonen i toppkarakterer fører til mye dum tidsbruk
headline_en: Inflation in top grades leads to a lot of pointless time use
lead_en: The article argues that grade inflation in Norwegian upper secondary school creates unfairness and wastes students’ time, but says replacing coursework grades with more exams is not a good solution. It discusses how admissions pressure has intensified as top grades have become far more common.
justification: The article is mainly about schools, grading, and university admission within public education, which fits health/welfare/public services, with a secondary governance aspect about system design and fairness.

headline_no: Frykter at et nei til EU på strøm kan sette helseavtale med EU i fare
headline_en: Fears that a no to the EU on electricity could jeopardize a health agreement with the EU
lead_en: Norway’s health minister warns that delaying disputed EU energy directives could weaken Norway’s chances of securing a key health preparedness agreement with the EU. The article frames this as a broader question of Norway’s relationship with Europe and access to crisis cooperation.
justification: The article is primarily about Norway’s strategic relationship with the EU and how compliance in one policy area affects broader foreign-policy and security-related cooperation, with health as a secondary domain.

headline_no: MDG: – Trist om Ap lar seg presse
headline_en: MDG: Sad if Labour lets itself be pressured
lead_en: The Green Party warns that Labour may give in to pressure from the Centre Party and drop the Denmark power cable in talks to save the government. MDG argues that cutting foreign power links would hurt Norway economically, increase vulnerability, and complicate climate policy.
justification: The article is primarily about electricity prices, power cables, and energy-market policy, while secondarily involving coalition bargaining and government survival.

headline_no: Fortsetter å samle inn milliarder
headline_en: Continues to raise billions
lead_en: Although the election is over, Donald Trump is still raising huge sums of money. The article explains that this war chest could help him maintain control over the Republican Party and strengthen his influence in Congress during his presidency.
justification: The article is primarily about political fundraising, party control, and Trump’s governing power rather than a specific policy area.

headline_no: Donald Trump: Hvor mye betyr hans politikk for norsk økonomi? – USA har litt å si for strømregningen her hjemme, sier ekspert.
headline_en: Donald Trump: How much does his policy matter for the Norwegian economy? “The U.S. has some impact on the electricity bill here at home,” says expert.
lead_en: The article examines how Donald Trump’s trade and energy policies could affect Norway through tariffs, interest rates, inflation, and electricity prices. Norwegian businesses fear the downside risks, though increased U.S. gas exports could eventually lower power prices in Europe and Norway.
justification: The article primarily focuses on how Trump’s policies may affect Norway’s economy, including trade, inflation, interest rates, and household costs, with a secondary foreign-policy dimension tied to EU-US relations and energy.

headline_no: Oljefondet offentliggjør tekstmeldinger
headline_en: The Oil Fund releases text messages
lead_en: After a new review, Norway’s sovereign wealth fund released Elon Musk’s text messages to Nicolai Tangen. The messages show Musk pressuring Tangen after the fund voted against his Tesla pay package, and the case centers on transparency and the fund’s public accountability.
justification: The article is primarily about governance, transparency, and leadership in the management of Norway’s sovereign wealth fund, with a secondary economic angle through the fund’s role as a major investor.

headline_no: Putins menn jaget ham ut av landet
headline_en: Putin’s men drove him out of the country
lead_en: The article profiles Russian lawyer Ivan Pavlov, who spent decades defending people accused of treason before the authorities turned on him as well. It describes how repression of defense lawyers has intensified in Russia and forced him into exile.
justification: The article is primarily about repression, state security, and Russia’s authoritarian political-legal system, with a Norway link through Pavlov’s past defense of a Bellona-related case.

headline_no: Familie på fem må hver uke spise 25 kilo av dette, ifølge myndighetenes anbefalinger
headline_en: Family of five would have to eat 25 kilos of this each week, according to the authorities’ recommendations
lead_en: The article illustrates how much fruit and vegetables a Norwegian family of five would need to eat to follow the national dietary guidelines. It uses the family’s experiment to show both the scale of the recommendation and the public-health rationale behind it.
justification: The article is primarily about public health guidance, diet, and preventive health policy, which fits the health/welfare/public services category.

headline_no: Mangemillionær etter å ha fristet gamere til å gamble
headline_en: Multi-millionaire after luring gamers into gambling
lead_en: The article examines how a Norwegian influencer made huge sums by promoting unregulated gambling linked to video games. It highlights concerns that such platforms recruit young players and operate without effective safeguards in Norway.
justification: The article’s main focus is unregulated online gambling tied to gaming and its social harms, which does not fit the main election issue categories, with a secondary governance angle about weak regulatory oversight.

headline_no: Kjøpte vaksine i julegave til nevøene
headline_en: Bought a vaccine as a Christmas gift for the nephews
lead_en: The article examines criticism of Norway’s decision to keep offering the cheaper HPV vaccine in the childhood immunization program instead of a broader but more expensive alternative. Doctors worry this could create health inequality because families who can pay privately may secure wider protection for their children.
justification: The article is primarily about vaccination policy and public health, with a secondary redistribution angle because cost differences may produce unequal access based on families’ ability to pay.

headline_no: Byrådet vil sprenge i torskens gyteområde
headline_en: The city government wants to blast in the cod’s spawning area
lead_en: The article reports that Oslo’s city government wants underwater blasting and dredging in a nationally important cod spawning ground to expand port capacity for a carbon-capture project. Critics call it hypocritical given recent calls to protect marine life in the Oslo Fjord.
justification: The article is primarily about environmental protection versus climate infrastructure, centering on Oslo Fjord ecology, marine habitat damage, and carbon-capture policy.

headline_no: Helsebyråden ber etaten rydde opp på legevakten
headline_en: The health city councillor asks the agency to clean up the emergency clinic
lead_en: The article reports that Oslo’s new emergency clinic has struggled with long waiting times and overcrowded waiting areas despite promises of a more efficient system. The city’s health councillor is now demanding improvements, including staff training and new AI-supported tools.
justification: The article is mainly about healthcare service delivery and problems at Oslo’s emergency clinic, with a secondary governance aspect related to management failures and administrative fixes.

headline_no: Mener det ligger en «pengenes forbandelse» over Norge
headline_en: Says there is a “curse of money” hanging over Norway
lead_en: The article reports on a Danish editor’s argument that Norway’s immense oil and gas wealth has made its politics complacent and morally poorer. It ties that critique to Norway’s Ukraine support and to a debate over whether the country uses its resources responsibly.
justification: The article is primarily about political leadership, priorities, and how Norwegian governments manage national wealth, with a secondary foreign-policy angle tied to criticism of Norway’s support for Ukraine.

headline_no: Mener det ligger en «pengenes forbandelse» over Norge
headline_en: Says there is a “curse of money” hanging over Norway
lead_en: The article reports on a Danish editor’s argument that Norway’s immense oil and gas wealth has made its politics complacent and morally poorer. It links that critique to Norway’s Ukraine support and to a debate over whether the country uses its resources responsibly.
justification: The article is primarily about political leadership, priorities, and how Norwegian governments manage national wealth, with a secondary foreign-policy angle tied to criticism of Norway’s support for Ukraine.

headline_no: Rødgrønn pionér endte som landssviker
headline_en: Red-green pioneer ended up as a traitor
lead_en: The article revisits former prime minister Jens Hundseid, portraying him as an early bridge between the agrarian movement and Labour before his later disgrace as a wartime collaborator. It uses his story to reflect on long-term political ties between the Centre Party and Labour.
justification: The article is primarily about party history, leadership, and coalition dynamics between the Centre Party and Labour rather than a substantive policy issue.

headline_no: Riktig å bruke makt for lærlinger
headline_en: Right to use leverage for apprentices
lead_en: The government is tightening rules so companies doing public contracts must use apprentices more actively, with at least 10 percent of the work to be carried out by them. The aim is to stop token use of apprentices and expand access to training places.
justification: The article mainly concerns vocational training and public provision of education/workforce development, while framing the Labour-led government’s tougher apprenticeship rules favourably.

headline_no: Den som vinner teknologikrigen, vinner verden
headline_en: Whoever wins the technology war wins the world
lead_en: The article argues that artificial intelligence has become central to great-power competition and that China has nearly erased the U.S. lead. Deepseek’s latest launch is presented as a geopolitical shock that could reshape the balance of power.
justification: The piece is primarily about geopolitical rivalry, national power, and military-strategic implications of AI competition between the United States and China.

headline_no: Må selv betale for å få rettet opp feil hos Skatteetaten
headline_en: Must pay themselves to correct errors at the Tax Administration
lead_en: More than 100 municipalities use imprecise tax valuation figures for property tax, and homeowners must often detect the errors themselves. To get the valuation corrected, they may also have to pay for documentation such as an appraisal or valuation report.
justification: The article is primarily about property valuation and taxation burdens on homeowners, with a secondary governance angle concerning administrative errors and how Skatteetaten handles corrections.

headline_no: Advarer om reell fare for sabotasjeangrep mot Norge
headline_en: Warns of a real risk of sabotage attacks against Norway
lead_en: Norway’s domestic security service warns that the risk of Russian sabotage against Norwegian critical infrastructure has increased. The National Security Authority says it is no longer a question of if such an attack will happen, but when.
justification: The article is primarily about national security threats, Russian hybrid warfare, and the vulnerability of Norwegian critical infrastructure.

headline_no: Vy-sjefen får en kvart million årligg fra staten – for å kjøre bil
headline_en: The Vy chief gets a quarter of a million kroner annually from the state – to drive a car
lead_en: Top executives in several state-owned companies receive fixed annual car allowances worth hundreds of thousands of kroner, on top of their salaries. The article questions whether these benefits are reasonable and transparent when funded by taxpayers.
justification: The article is mainly about governance, oversight, and transparency in state-owned companies’ executive compensation, framed critically toward the government’s handling of publicly funded pay practices.

headline_no: Trump tilbyr sluttpakker til millioner av statsansatte. Bak ligger en svært radikal idé.
headline_en: Trump offers severance packages to millions of public employees. Behind it lies a highly radical idea.
lead_en: Millions of U.S. federal employees have been offered severance packages as part of a sweeping effort to shrink the state and remove officials seen as obstacles to Trump’s agenda. The article links the move to a radical anti-bureaucratic ideology promoted by Vice President J.D. Vance and thinkers on the American right.
justification: The article is primarily about state restructuring, executive power, and ideological attacks on the bureaucracy, which fits governance and leadership rather than a specific policy domain.

headline_no: Trump ønsker ikke transpersoner i militæret
headline_en: Trump does not want transgender people in the military
lead_en: Donald Trump has ordered the Pentagon to review its rules on transgender personnel in the armed forces, a move that could lead to them being removed from service. The measure is justified by the administration as necessary for military readiness, but is already facing opposition and likely legal challenges.
justification: The article is primarily about military policy and defence personnel rules, with a secondary governance dimension related to executive orders and legal conflict.

headline_no: Donald Trumps mest sårbare kandidater skal i ilden samtidig
headline_en: Donald Trump’s most vulnerable nominees face the test at the same time
lead_en: Three of Donald Trump’s most controversial cabinet and security nominees are facing Senate hearings on the same day. The article examines their backgrounds, why they are contested, and whether they are likely to be confirmed.
justification: The article is mainly about appointments, confirmation battles, and political leadership in the incoming U.S. administration, with a secondary security angle because several nominees are for intelligence and law-enforcement roles.

headline_no: Støre kjemper mot Sp. Brenna kjemper mot Støre.
headline_en: Støre is fighting the Centre Party. Brenna is fighting Støre.
lead_en: Several sources in the government apparatus say the dispute with the Centre Party over electricity policy is being used in Labour’s internal power struggle. The article portrays Tonje Brenna and her allies as challenging Jonas Gahr Støre’s leadership during a politically damaging conflict.
justification: The article is primarily about Labour’s internal leadership conflict, party strategy, and coalition tensions, with electricity policy serving as a secondary backdrop.

headline_no: Svært høyt spill i Arbeiderpartiet
headline_en: Very high-stakes game in the Labour Party
lead_en: As Jonas Gahr Støre battles the Centre Party over energy cooperation with the EU, he is also being undermined by forces inside his own party. The article frames Tonje Brenna’s camp as putting internal leadership ambitions ahead of Norway’s broader interests.
justification: The article is primarily about internal Labour leadership conflict and party power struggles, with the energy/EU dispute as a secondary policy context.

headline_no: Kollisjonskurs etter møtemaraton
headline_en: On a collision course after a marathon of meetings
lead_en: The conflict between Labour and the Centre Party over EU energy directives remains deadlocked after intensive meetings. Støre wants to preserve the coalition, while Vedum argues that current electricity policy and closer ties to the EU have harmed Norway.
justification: The article is primarily about electricity prices and Norway’s energy policy toward the EU, with a secondary governance dimension because the dispute threatens the survival of the governing coalition.

headline_no: Var hans siste (spørre)time kommet?
headline_en: Had his last question time come?
lead_en: The article uses Erling Sande’s appearance in parliamentary question time to illustrate the government drama over the EU energy dispute. It suggests that the Centre Party’s ministers could soon be out of office if the coalition conflict with Labour ends in a break.
justification: The article is primarily about government survival, cabinet positions, and coalition conflict, while the underlying policy backdrop is the electricity and EU energy dispute.

headline_no: Arbeiderpartiet kritisk til Mehls forslag om visitasjonssoner
headline_en: Labour critical of Mehl’s proposal for stop-and-search zones
lead_en: Labour criticizes Justice Minister Emilie Enger Mehl’s proposal to let police search people without specific suspicion, saying it has not been discussed with the party and conflicts with legal principles. The article presents the idea as politically divisive and weakly grounded in police advice.
justification: The article mainly concerns policing and crime policy through the debate over stop-and-search zones, while also showing Labour in a favourable light for opposing the measure on rule-of-law grounds.

headline_no: De amerikanske teknologiselskapene fyller Oljefondet
headline_en: American technology companies are filling the Oil Fund
lead_en: Norway’s sovereign wealth fund earned enormous returns from major U.S. tech stocks in 2024, with Nvidia alone contributing more than 250 billion kroner. The article also notes recent losses and renewed attention to Nicolai Tangen’s communication with Elon Musk.
justification: The article is primarily about the sovereign wealth fund’s investments and returns, which fits the Oljefondet category, with a secondary economic angle due to market performance and financial gains.

headline_no: Må betale millionerstatning til piloter
headline_en: Must pay millions in compensation to pilots
lead_en: An arbitration case in Denmark concluded that the airline failed to follow an agreement on pilots’ reemployment rights after pandemic-era layoffs. Norwegian has been ordered to pay compensation, though the amount is lower than it would have been under Norwegian law.
justification: The article is mainly about a labor and arbitration dispute over pilot reemployment rights, which does not fit the main election issue categories and is therefore coded as Other.

headline_no: Sykepleier: – Folk må beskytte seg
headline_en: Nurse: People must protect themselves
lead_en: A nurse warns people to use ice grips and take precautions in slippery conditions, as falls on ice are causing many injuries and putting extra pressure on emergency care and hospitals. She also urges Oslo municipality to improve gritting of walkways and pavements.
justification: The article is primarily about injury prevention and pressure on health services from ice-related falls, with a secondary governance angle concerning municipal responsibility for safer public walkways.

headline_no: Tragedie på festival med millioner
headline_en: Tragedy at festival attended by millions
lead_en: At least 30 people were killed and 90 injured in a crowd crush during the Kumbh Mela festival in India. The article describes how panic and weak crowd control led to pilgrims being trampled during a ritual gathering.
justification: The article is an international disaster report about a deadly crowd crush at a religious festival and does not fit the main Norwegian election issue categories.

section_no: Nyheter | Børsen I Gvr | Saksøker Staten
headline_no: – Breivik er fremdeles farlig
headline_en: Breivik is still dangerous
lead_en: Anders Behring Breivik has again lost in court in his attempt to challenge the conditions of his imprisonment. The appeals court found that the strict security measures and isolation remain justified and do not violate his human rights.
justification: The article is primarily about a court case over prison conditions and human rights for Anders Behring Breivik, which falls outside the main election issue categories, with a secondary governance angle related to the state and legal system.

headline_no: Lykke til alene, Arbeiderpartiet
headline_en: Good luck on your own, Labour Party
lead_en: Prime Minister Jonas Gahr Støre wanted a three-party government after the 2021 election, but that never happened; now the two-party government of Labour and the Centre Party has also collapsed. The trigger was the EU clean energy package, which had long been a political time bomb under the coalition.
justification: The article is primarily about the collapse of the Labour–Centre coalition and Støre’s leadership choice, making governance the main issue, with a secondary foreign policy/EU security dimension.

headline_no: Noen vil bli fryktelig rike på Trumps metode
headline_en: Some people will become extremely rich from Trump’s method
lead_en: Donald Trump may be able to give the U.S. economy a short-term boost, but the columnist argues that his approach resembles a giant “pump and dump” scheme. The gains would mainly enrich the wealthy, while the broader public could be left with the losses later.
justification: The article is primarily about Trump’s economic policy, deregulation, tariffs, and speculative growth effects, with taxation and distributional consequences as a secondary theme.

headline_no: Koranbrenner drept, fem pågrepet
headline_en: Quran burner killed, five arrested
lead_en: Salwan Momika, known in Sweden for burning Qurans, was reportedly shot dead near Stockholm. The article also notes that he applied for asylum in Norway last year, was rejected, and said being returned to Sweden was like a death sentence.
justification: The article is primarily about asylum, deportation, and migration-related legal status, with a secondary security angle tied to NATO tensions and terrorism concerns.

headline_no: Drap på koranbrenner er et nederlag for ytringsfriheten
headline_en: The killing of a Quran burner is a defeat for freedom of expression
lead_en: The killing of Salwan Momika reinforces a broader pattern in which freedom of expression is being weakened. The article also notes that the case matters in Norway because Momika sought asylum there and was later returned to Sweden.
justification: The article is primarily an opinion piece about freedom of expression and the limits of protest and religious offence, which does not fit the main campaign issue categories, though it has a secondary security dimension.

headline_no: Fire omkom, Alta i dyp sorg
headline_en: Four dead, Alta in deep mourning
lead_en: Four people from a group connected to Alta Folk High School were confirmed dead after an avalanche in the French Alps. The school and Alta municipality say they are in deep mourning and are providing crisis support to students and relatives.
justification: The article is a straight news report about a fatal avalanche involving Norwegians abroad and does not primarily concern any of the defined election issue categories.

headline_no: «Et parti i krise har endelig funnet en sak de kan samles om»
headline_en: “A party in crisis has finally found an issue they can unite around”
lead_en: It became clear on Thursday afternoon that the Centre Party is leaving the government after weeks of disagreement with Labour over the EU’s clean energy package. The break means eight ministers will leave, and Jonas Gahr Støre will now form a new government.
justification: The article is primarily about coalition breakdown, cabinet reshuffling, and Støre’s position as prime minister, with energy policy as a secondary substantive issue.

headline_no: Listhaug: – Jeg kan knapt huske en verre regjering
headline_en: Listhaug: “I can hardly remember a worse government”
lead_en: After the government break-up, opposition parties describe the Labour–Centre coalition as chaotic and unsuccessful, while parties on the left want to pull the new Labour government further left. Sylvi Listhaug and Erna Solberg both sharply criticize the outgoing government.
justification: The article is primarily about party reactions, government breakdown, and electoral positioning around Støre’s new minority government, with energy and cost-of-living policy as a secondary issue.

headline_no: Ofrer alt i håp om å vinne noe
headline_en: Sacrificing everything in the hope of winning something
lead_en: The article argues that Trygve Slagsvold Vedum is giving up government power over the dispute on EU energy directives, hoping to recover voters in opposition. It presents the break as a strategic gamble that may benefit Labour more than the Centre Party.
justification: The article is primarily about the government split, party strategy, and leadership consequences for Labour and the Centre Party, with electricity and energy policy as the secondary issue.

headline_no: For andre gang i norsk historie går et parti ut av regjering. Hva betyr det?
headline_en: For the second time in Norwegian history, a party leaves the government. What does it mean?
lead_en: After three and a half years, the Centre Party has left Støre’s government, and the article explains what this means in practice. It outlines how Labour will replace the departing ministers and how the minority government will operate going forward.
justification: The article is primarily an explainer on government breakdown, cabinet replacement, and parliamentary consequences, which makes governance the clear main issue.

headline_no: Dømt for sovevoldtekt, kjemper for å bli frikjent
headline_en: Convicted of rape of a sleeping person, fights to be acquitted
lead_en: Authorities do not expect to find any survivors after a passenger plane collided with a military helicopter and crashed into the Potomac River in Washington, D.C. Norwegian authorities are also checking whether any Norwegian citizens were affected.
justification: The article is a news report about an air disaster in the United States with a limited Norwegian connection, and it does not primarily fit any of the defined election issue categories.

headline_no: Onkel til «Den kurdiske reven» drept i Sverige
headline_en: Uncle of “The Kurdish Fox” killed in Sweden
lead_en: Rawa Majid’s uncle was shot dead north of Stockholm, and Swedish police arrested one person after an exchange of fire. The article notes that Majid leads the Foxtrot criminal network and that the killing could trigger further retaliation in gang circles.
justification: The article is primarily about gang violence and criminal retaliation in Sweden, which fits the crime dimension of the immigration/integration/crime category, with a secondary public security angle.

headline_no: Veien til «Farmen kjendis» går gjennom å lage bråk
headline_en: The road to “Farmen kjend is” goes through causing trouble
lead_en: The article argues that influencers thrive on attention, often negative, and that creating controversy can be a path to greater fame and TV opportunities. It presents influencer culture as commercially driven and highly influential among young people in Norway.
justification: The article is a cultural commentary on influencers, media attention, and celebrity reality TV, which does not primarily fit any of the defined political issue categories.

headline_no: Klimaaktivister vant mot Equinor
headline_en: Climate activists won against Equinor
lead_en: Equinor has lost the approval for the controversial Rosebank oil field in the UK because the climate impact from burning the oil and gas was not properly assessed. The ruling may also have consequences for several Norwegian oil and gas fields facing similar legal challenges.
justification: The article is primarily about climate law, oil development, and the environmental consequences of fossil fuel projects, making climate and oil policy the dominant issue.

headline_no: Zaniar Matapour trekker anken
headline_en: Zaniar Matapour withdraws his appeal
lead_en: The man convicted of terrorism says he was not able to prepare properly for the appeal case, so he is withdrawing it. As a result, his 30-year preventive detention sentence for the 25 June 2022 Oslo shooting remains in place.
justification: The article is primarily about a convicted terrorist, the aftermath of the 25 June attack in Oslo, and legal proceedings related to a terrorism case, which fits the security category.

headline_no: Lønnsoppgjøret kan hindre rentekutt
headline_en: The wage settlement could prevent rate cuts
lead_en: Most signs suggest households in Norway will have more purchasing power in 2025, but that may also reduce the chances of interest-rate cuts. Economists warn that a costly wage settlement could keep inflationary pressure up and lead to fewer cuts from Norges Bank.
justification: The article is primarily about wages, inflation, interest rates, and household purchasing power in Norway, which squarely fits the economy and cost-of-living category.

headline_no: Justisministeren svarer Hadia Tajik (Ap)
headline_en: The justice minister responds to Hadia Tajik (Labour)
lead_en: Justice Minister Emilie Enger Mehl defends her proposal to let police search people without individual suspicion in limited geographic areas. She argues the government must consider stronger tools to stay ahead of organized crime, after criticism from Labour’s Hadia Tajik.
justification: The article is primarily about anti-gang crime policy and police powers, which fits the crime dimension of IMM, while the dispute within government gives it a secondary governance element.

headline_no: Selvmordsforsker rystet etter TV-serie
headline_en: Suicide researcher shaken after TV series
lead_en: Assisted suicide is illegal in Norway, yet a new TV 2 documentary suggests that some people are still offering to help vulnerable individuals die. A suicide researcher calls this frightening and morally reprehensible, warning that people in crisis are especially vulnerable to manipulation.
justification: The article is primarily about suicide, end-of-life ethics, and gaps in mental health support, which places it closest to health and welfare, with a secondary criminal/legal dimension outside the main issue set.

headline_no: Video av knivstikking spres i sosiale medier
headline_en: Video of stabbing spreads on social media
lead_en: Two 16-year-olds are in custody and a third has been charged after a stabbing at a metro station in Oslo. Police are urging people to stop sharing video of the brutal attack, which they believe is linked to conflict between local youth environments.
justification: The article is primarily about youth violence, attempted murder, and gang-like conflict in Oslo, which fits the crime dimension of the immigration/integration/crime category.

headline_no: Har Ukrainas droneangrep effekt på krigen?
headline_en: Are Ukraine’s drone attacks having an effect on the war?
lead_en: Ukraine is intensifying its drone war against Russia in the hope of pressuring Putin before any peace negotiations. The article examines whether repeated strikes on Russian oil facilities are actually weakening Russia’s war effort.
justification: The article is primarily about the war in Ukraine, Russian military vulnerability, and the strategic effect of drone strikes, which clearly places it in the security/foreign policy category.

headline_no: Her glemmer hun jobben og rampete unger
headline_en: Here she forgets her job and mischievous kids
lead_en: Even a few seconds in icy water can improve mood and have positive effects on the body, according to researchers. The article follows a Norwegian ice bather and presents claimed health benefits as well as the risks of cold-water bathing.
justification: The article is primarily about health effects and risks of ice bathing, which fits the health and welfare category.

headline_no: Forslaget om visitasjonssoner bør lide samme skjebne som Mehl
headline_en: The proposal for stop-and-search zones should suffer the same fate as Mehl
lead_en: Justice Minister Emilie Enger Mehl’s proposal to let police search people in certain areas without specific suspicion was among her last initiatives before the government coalition collapsed. The article argues that the proposal should be dropped because it is intrusive, weakly evidenced, and unnecessary.
justification: The article’s main focus is policing, crime prevention, and suspicionless searches in high-crime urban areas, which fits immigration/integration/crime, with a secondary governance angle because it is tied to Mehl’s exit as minister.

headline_no: Finalen i «Maktorama»
headline_en: The finale of “Powerorama”
lead_en: The piece satirically compares the collapse of the Labour–Centre Party government relationship to reality TV. It asks whether Jonas Gahr Støre now stands more securely after the breakup and the move to a one-party Labour government.
justification: The article is primarily about government breakdown, leadership, coalition dynamics, and Støre’s position, making governance the dominant issue, with energy and electricity disputes as a secondary theme.

headline_no: Obos bygger den omstridte O-en
headline_en: Obos is building the controversial O
lead_en: Housing construction in Oslo has nearly stalled, but Obos is now restarting several projects, including the controversial ring-shaped building at Ammerud. The article presents this as a sign of cautious recovery in the housing market despite weak purchasing power and high costs.
justification: The article is mainly about the housing market, construction slowdown, buyer purchasing power, and broader economic conditions affecting new residential projects.

headline_no: Flere får påvist svineinfluensa. Ifølge FHI finnes den ikke.
headline_en: More people are being diagnosed with swine flu. According to the FHI, it no longer exists.
lead_en: As flu season gets underway, some parents are being told their children have “swine flu,” but the Norwegian Institute of Public Health says this is misleading. Researchers explain that it is actually the ordinary H1N1 influenza strain now circulating seasonally, not a new pandemic threat.
justification: The article is primarily about public health communication, influenza diagnosis, and vaccination information, which fits the health category.

headline_no: Hun ble enke og er rasende på Vesten
headline_en: She became a widow and is furious with the West
lead_en: Ukrainian architect Anna Kyrii, whose husband was killed in the war, says the war has taught her that Ukrainians can rely only on themselves. The article describes growing anger in Ukraine toward Western countries for delayed and insufficient support, while also noting that Norway is among the countries that have contributed.
justification: The article is primarily about the war in Ukraine, Western support, NATO/European security, and Norway’s role within that broader foreign-policy and defence context.

headline_no: Dødsdjevlene
headline_en: The death devils
lead_en: The article argues that deeply desperate people are being treated in an undignified way through illegal assisted-suicide networks. Using TV 2’s documentary as a starting point, it calls for Norwegian politicians to seriously examine the issue of assisted dying.
justification: The primary focus is assisted dying, mental illness, healthcare, and access to dignified end-of-life care, with a secondary governance element because it calls for political and legal review.

headline_no: Fikk bot selv om han sto på oppmerket plass
headline_en: He was fined even though he was parked in a marked space
lead_en: An Oslo man with a disability parking permit was fined after parking in a spot marked as accessible both on the ground and on the municipality’s website. The case highlights confusing municipal signage and administrative handling after the space had effectively been moved.
justification: The article is mainly a local administrative and consumer-style dispute over parking enforcement and misleading municipal information, with a secondary link to accessibility/public services.

headline_no: Dette er den skjulte norske slummen
headline_en: This is Norway’s hidden slum
lead_en: Aftenposten reveals severe labour-law violations and dangerous living conditions for seasonal workers harvesting Norwegian produce sold with the “Nyt Norge” label. The investigation shows repeated abuses, lack of transparency, and continued problems for migrant farm workers across Norway.
justification: The article mainly concerns vulnerable migrant seasonal workers, their exploitation and living conditions, which fits immigration/integration/crime, with a secondary public-health and welfare aspect due to the unsafe housing conditions.

headline_no: Slaget står om finansministeren. Disse kan bli nye Ap-statsråder.
headline_en: The battle is over the finance minister. These could become Labour’s new ministers.
lead_en: After the Centre Party left the government, attention in Labour has turned to who Jonas Gahr Støre will appoint as finance minister and to other cabinet posts. The article frames the choice as strategically important because the finance minister could also emerge as Støre’s eventual successor as party leader.
justification: The article is chiefly about cabinet formation, internal Labour succession, and leadership strategy after a government reshuffle, which squarely fits governance and leadership.

headline_no: Dette vil «norgespris» på strøm bety for deg
headline_en: This is what the “Norway price” for electricity will mean for you
lead_en: Labour is introducing an optional fixed-price electricity scheme at 40 øre per kWh, plus VAT, alongside a cut in grid-fee VAT. The article explains how the plan could lower and stabilize power costs for many households, especially in southern Norway.
justification: The article is primarily about electricity prices and household cost relief through Labour’s new fixed-price power policy, with a secondary governance angle because it is presented as a government policy initiative.

headline_no: Løp og kjøp! (Etter valget, da)
headline_en: Run and buy! (After the election, that is)
lead_en: The commentary argues that Labour’s new subsidized fixed-price electricity plan could undercut the Centre Party and other opposition parties in the election campaign. It says the policy gives voters a simple answer to high power prices, even if it creates problematic incentives in the energy system.
justification: The article is primarily about electricity prices and Labour’s new power-price policy, with a secondary governance angle because it emphasizes the electoral and strategic impact on party competition.

headline_no: Dette skjedde da fire norske døde
headline_en: This is what happened when four Norwegians died
lead_en: Four Norwegians died in a massive avalanche in the French Alps while taking part in an avalanche course linked to Alta Folk High School. The article reconstructs the accident and the rescue operation after three companions witnessed the mountainside collapse.
justification: The article is a factual report on a fatal avalanche accident involving Norwegians abroad and does not primarily fit any of the specified election-related policy categories.

headline_no: Første stopp for USAs nye utenriksminister: Panamakanalen
headline_en: First stop for America’s new foreign minister: the Panama Canal
lead_en: As Marco Rubio begins his first trip as U.S. secretary of state, attention is focused on Donald Trump’s claims about Chinese influence over the Panama Canal. The article explains that China does not control the canal, but examines why U.S. officials still see Chinese-linked port operations there as a security concern.
justification: The article is primarily about foreign policy, geopolitical rivalry, and security concerns surrounding U.S., Chinese, and Panamanian control and influence over the canal.

headline_no: Absolutt alt med Donald Trump er feil
headline_en: Absolutely everything about Donald Trump is wrong
lead_en: The commentary argues that Donald Trump’s style, rhetoric, and grandiosity clash completely with Scandinavian political norms. Using the idea of the Jante Law, it says Trump should forget about taking Greenland and learn some humility instead.
justification: The article is primarily about Trump, Greenland, and international politics from a Scandinavian/Norwegian perspective, placing it under foreign policy and security, with a secondary cultural/commentary element.

headline_no: Fra «hotellmaskin i en gipsboks» til bygg med sjel
headline_en: From a “hotel machine in a plaster box” to a building with soul
lead_en: A historic Oslo hotel has been fully renovated to make its interior match its stately exterior, with a strong emphasis on reuse and sustainability. During the renovation, workers also uncovered an old bank vault hidden behind a wall.
justification: The article mainly emphasizes reuse, sustainability, and reducing waste in renovation, which fits climate/environment, with a secondary cultural and lifestyle angle.

headline_no: Politiet: Døren til bygget sto åpen
headline_en: Police: The door to the building was open
lead_en: Police are investigating several possible causes of the Oslo apartment-building fire, including technical failure, accident, or arson. One confirmed detail is that the building’s door was open when police arrived, while the property owner says there was no reason it should have been.
justification: The article is primarily a local crime and fire-investigation report that does not fit the main election issue categories, with a secondary governance angle because it involves municipal permits and official investigations.

headline_no: Mistanke om grovt skadeverk på fiberkabel
headline_en: Suspected major sabotage of fiber-optic cable
lead_en: Norwegian police brought the Norwegian-owned cargo ship Silver Dania into Tromsø after Latvian authorities requested assistance in investigating damage to a fiber-optic cable in the Baltic Sea. The case is being treated as suspected serious vandalism, against a backdrop of wider concern over attacks on undersea infrastructure.
justification: The article is primarily about suspected damage to critical undersea infrastructure, NATO monitoring, and Norway’s role in a cross-border security investigation, which fits security/defence/foreign policy.

headline_no: «Sjekk deg» for HPV hjemme: – Et fint supplement
headline_en: “Check yourself” for HPV at home: “A good supplement”
lead_en: Norway has introduced home HPV tests through GP offices as an alternative for women who avoid gynecological exams, and early results suggest it is reaching many who have not tested in years. Health professionals describe it as a useful supplement, especially for women who find ordinary screening difficult.
justification: The article is primarily about cancer screening, preventive healthcare, and access to medical testing, which fits the health/public services category.

headline_no: MDG vil ha mobilfrie stillevogner på T-banen
headline_en: The Greens want mobile-free quiet carriages on the metro
lead_en: MDG wants Oslo’s metro to test mobile-free quiet carriages so passengers can travel in silence. The proposal is presented as a quality-of-life measure, but both Sporveien and the city’s transport councillor doubt it would work in practice.
justification: The article is mainly about a local public-transport proposal that does not fit the main election issue categories, with a secondary governance aspect because it centers on a party proposal and city-level political debate.

headline_no: Klar for nye regjeringskriser
headline_en: Ready for new government crises
lead_en: The commentator argues that Norway’s disputes over EU energy policy are far from over, and that the issue is likely to trigger new government crises for years to come. Labour and the Centre Party are now forced to sharpen their profiles after the coalition breakdown.
justification: The article is primarily about government stability, party strategy, and coalition conflict over energy policy, with energy prices and EU electricity policy as the secondary substantive issue.

headline_no: «Ikke kjent med» at strømplan har vaert behandlet
headline_en: “Not aware” that the electricity plan has been formally processed
lead_en: The day after the Centre Party left government, Støre presented a new electricity policy including a fixed “Norway price” for power. But according to the Finance Ministry’s political leadership, the proposal had not gone through formal government processing.
justification: The article primarily questions the government’s competence and decision-making procedures in launching a major power policy, with electricity policy as a secondary issue.

headline_no: Mintes de fire omkomne elevene
headline_en: The four deceased students were remembered
lead_en: A memorial was held in Lanslebourg, France, for the four Norwegian students who died in an avalanche during a course trip from Alta Folk High School. Relatives, school representatives, the Norwegian ambassador, and the Seamen’s Church took part.
justification: The article is a human-interest news report about a fatal accident involving Norwegian citizens abroad, not a political or campaign-related policy issue.

headline_no: Gledesscener da tre israelske gisler og over 180 palestinske fanger ble løslatt
headline_en: Scenes of joy as three Israeli hostages and more than 180 Palestinian prisoners were released
lead_en: Three Israeli hostages and 183 Palestinian prisoners were released in a new exchange under the Gaza ceasefire arrangement. Families in Ramallah and Israel waited anxiously as the handover unfolded amid continued tensions and uncertainty.
justification: The article centers on the Gaza war and hostage-prisoner exchanges between Israel and Hamas, which fits the Gaza/Palestine category.

headline_no: Stenger statsansatte ute fra It-systemer
headline_en: Blocking government employees from IT systems
lead_en: Elon Musk’s team at the U.S. federal HR agency has blocked senior civil servants from systems containing sensitive personnel data on millions of federal employees. Officials say the move has created alarm over oversight, cybersecurity, and political control of the bureaucracy.
justification: The article is primarily about state administration, control of the bureaucracy, and governance under Trump and Musk rather than a specific policy domain.

headline_no: Beordrer kartlegging av Fbi-etterforskere
headline_en: Orders mapping of FBI investigators
lead_en: The Trump administration has asked for the names of all FBI employees who worked on January 6 cases and has already dismissed prosecutors involved in those prosecutions. The move is presented as part of a broader effort to purge officials seen as insufficiently loyal to Trump.
justification: The article is primarily about political control of state institutions, dismissals in the justice system, and administrative power under Trump, which fits governance and leadership.

headline_no: Retten lot ham gå. Dermed kunne dødshjelperen holde på som før.
headline_en: The court let him go. That allowed the assisted-death helper to continue as before.
lead_en: A man previously convicted in Norway for assisting suicide was allowed to remain free pending appeal, and is now jailed in Sweden on suspicion of murder after a TV 2 investigation. The article questions how he was able to continue operating despite earlier warnings and convictions.
justification: The article is primarily a crime and justice report about assisted suicide and alleged murder, which does not fit the main election issue categories, with a secondary governance angle about court and prosecutorial decisions.

headline_no: Norske diplomater i området da Odesa ble angrepet
headline_en: Norwegian diplomats were in the area when Odesa was attacked
lead_en: Russian attacks hit several Ukrainian cities, and Norway’s foreign ministry confirmed that Norwegian embassy personnel were in the attacked area in Odesa but were unharmed. The strikes also hit residential buildings and energy infrastructure, prompting renewed calls from Zelenskyj for stronger air defence.
justification: The article is primarily about the war in Ukraine, attacks on civilian and energy targets, and Norway’s diplomatic presence in the conflict zone, which fits security/foreign policy.

headline_no: Bilfører siktet for drapsforsøk
headline_en: Driver charged with attempted murder
lead_en: A driver has been charged with attempted murder after a violent incident in Greåker, Sarpsborg, where two people were struck by a car. Police say four people are listed as victims, but the extent of the injuries is still unclear.
justification: The article is a local crime report about a violent incident and attempted murder charge, which does not fit the main political issue categories.

headline_no: Det norske kompromisset er i ferd med å tape terreng
headline_en: The Norwegian compromise is starting to lose ground
lead_en: The article argues that Norway is increasingly being pulled along by EU integration while public support for the EEA compromise is weakening. The recent government split over EU energy directives is presented as a sign of deeper political conflict to come.
justification: The article is primarily about political conflict over the EEA, party competition, and the stability of Norway’s governing compromise with the EU, making governance the dominant issue.

headline_no: Brennhett boligmarked
headline_en: Red-hot housing market
lead_en: Oslo’s housing market is surging, especially for smaller starter homes, with unusually strong January price growth and intense competition at viewings. Banks are also reporting a sharp rise in mortgage pre-approval applications after changes to lending rules.
justification: The article is primarily about housing prices, borrowing conditions, and household economic pressure, which fits the economy/cost-of-living category.

headline_no: Farvel, Frankrike!
headline_en: Goodbye, France!
lead_en: France has handed over its last military base in Chad, marking another setback for French influence in Africa as several former colonies move closer to partners such as Russia, Turkey, and the UAE. The article frames this as part of a broader shift in regional security and postcolonial power relations.
justification: The article is primarily about military presence, shifting alliances, and geopolitical influence in Africa, which fits security, defence, and foreign policy.

headline_no: Kampen om etterkrigstiden
headline_en: The Battle Over the Postwar Era
lead_en: A debate about Norway's postwar period has reignited, with disagreement over whether it was economically successful. The discussion reveals how historical interpretations are shaped by contemporary political views.
justification: The article examines competing political and ideological narratives about postwar governance and economic policy, showing how different parties (Labour, Rødt, conservatives) use history to justify contemporary positions.

headline_no: Åpner for turistskatt for cruise- og bobilturister
headline_en: Opens Door to Tourist Tax for Cruise and Camper Tourists
lead_en: Oslo's centre-right city council rejected a general tourist tax on hotel guests but signaled openness to taxing cruise ship and camper van tourists, who the council argues consume city services without spending proportionally.
justification: The article centers on a debate over implementing tourist taxation in Oslo, with the centre-right council using tax policy arguments to distinguish between tourist groups—a taxation and governance issue.

headline_no: Putin tyr til tvang for å skaffe nok soldater
headline_en: Putin Resorts to Coercion to Secure Enough Soldiers
lead_en: Russia struggles to recruit soldiers for its war in Ukraine and is using increasingly drastic methods, including deception, debt relief, and forced conscription of prisoners and foreign nationals to fill military ranks.
justification: The article reports on Russia's military recruitment crisis and coercive tactics in its war against Ukraine, a core security and foreign policy issue directly relevant to Norwegian NATO interests.

headline_no: Norden gjør seg klar for luftkrig på kort varsel
headline_en: Nordic Region Prepares for Air Warfare at Short Notice
lead_en: Nordic air forces are establishing a unified command structure with 250 modern fighter jets to rapidly counter Russian threats across the region, representing a unique NATO integration effort centered in Norway.
justification: The article covers Nordic defence cooperation and NATO military integration in response to Russian aggression, a core security and foreign policy matter central to Norway's strategic position.

headline_no: Miljø-topp: Med «norgespris» kan smart strømstyring vaere bortkastet
headline_en: Environment Chief: With "Norway Price," Smart Power Management Could Be Wasted
lead_en: Environmental advocates warn that the government's proposed fixed electricity price of 40 øre per kilowatt-hour could undermine incentives for energy efficiency investments and smart power management that the state has spent billions supporting.
justification: The article debates the economic and environmental implications of a fixed electricity price policy, focusing on cost-of-living relief versus energy efficiency goals and climate policy coherence.

headline_no: Ingen førte referat fra oppvaskmøtet etter togkaoset
headline_en: No Minutes Taken from Meeting After Train Chaos
lead_en: After a nationwide train shutdown on Christmas Day stranded 60,000 passengers, Transport Minister Jon-ivar Nygård held a closed-door meeting with railway agencies but no official minutes were recorded, limiting public accountability.
justification: The article criticizes Labour minister Jon-ivar Nygård's lack of transparency and documentation regarding government handling of a major infrastructure failure, raising governance and accountability concerns.

headline_no: Nå er det i gang. Ingen er trygge.
headline_en: It's Begun. No One is Safe.
lead_en: Trump has announced tariffs of 25% on Canadian and Mexican goods and 10% on Chinese imports, potentially triggering a major trade war that threatens global economic stability and challenges traditional US alliances.
justification: The article analyzes Trump's tariff announcements as a security and geopolitical threat to the international order and US allies, with significant economic consequences relevant to Norway's trade and NATO relationships.

headline_no: Donald Trumps bråstart kan bli et tegn på svakhet
headline_en: Donald Trump's Fast Start Could Be a Sign of Weakness
lead_en: Trump has signed over 300 executive orders in his first week, but legal challenges, Republican defections, and constitutional constraints on presidential power could undermine his agenda and reveal vulnerabilities in his authority.
justification: The article analyzes Trump's domestic political position and institutional constraints on presidential power, relevant to Norway as a NATO ally dependent on US security commitments and policy stability.

headline_no: Kan landet få en Ap/H-regjering?
headline_en: Could the Country Get a Labour/Conservative Government?
lead_en: Labour and Conservative parties agree on most major issues, including EU policy, defence, and climate, raising the possibility of a "purple" coalition government—but political incentives and the need for a strong opposition argue against it.
justification: The article analyzes possible coalition governments after the 2025 election, including a potential Labour-Conservative partnership, focusing on government formation and political strategy.

headline_no: Han har master i IT, men får ikke jobb
headline_en: He Has a Master's in IT, But Can't Get a Job
lead_en: A recent graduate with a master's degree in programming has sent 100 job applications and attended 20 interviews without success, reflecting a broader trend where many new graduates struggle longer than expected to enter the workforce.
justification: The article examines employment challenges for recent graduates and education-to-work transition, touching on labour market conditions and welfare/public services related to education systems.

headline_no: Bryter avtaler og vingler om EU
headline_en: Breaks agreements and wavers on the EU
lead_en: The editorial criticizes the Centre Party for leaving government over EU directives and argues that Trygve Slagsvold Vedum is undermining trust by abandoning earlier commitments.
justification: The article is primarily about government responsibility, coalition breakdown, and party leadership over the EEA/EU dispute, while energy and economic policy are secondary.

headline_no: Lite eksport og mye innvandring skaper tysk mismot
headline_en: Weak exports and high immigration are creating German gloom
lead_en: The commentary argues that Germany’s election is shaped by both economic stagnation and immigration, and that these two problems are feeding each other politically.
justification: The article primarily focuses on Germany’s economic weakness, industry troubles, and labour market problems, while immigration is a major secondary issue in the election debate.

headline_no: Musk strupe bistands-gigant
headline_en: Musk moves to strangle aid giant
lead_en: Elon Musk says USAID should be shut down, raising fears of major consequences for global aid work and for Norwegian organizations that depend on U.S. funding.
justification: The article is mainly about U.S. foreign aid and the possible shutdown of USAID, which does not fit the main election issue categories, though government power and institutional change are secondary.

headline_no: Bruker milliarder på Ikt-løsning. Ansatte mener dagens system fungerer.
headline_en: Billions are being spent on an ICT solution. Employees say the current system works.
lead_en: A costly new ICT platform for Norway’s ministries is delayed and getting more expensive, while employees question why an existing system cannot be expanded instead.
justification: The article is mainly about public administration, state competence, and expensive government decision-making, with Labour-led government institutions framed critically over delays and costs.

headline_no: Ap holder døren på gløtt for vannkraft fra vernede vassdrag
headline_en: Labour leaves the door slightly open to hydropower from protected waterways
lead_en: Labour’s climate minister says he is open to discussing whether protected waterways could contribute more hydropower, triggering sharp criticism from the Greens.
justification: The article is mainly about the conflict between nature protection and expanding hydropower, with electricity supply and prices as a secondary economic issue.

headline_no: Assad-regimets fall
headline_en: The fall of the Assad regime
lead_en: In Idlib, where the rebels have ruled for years, the article explores what daily life, women’s rights, and Islamic law there may reveal about the future direction of a new Syria.
justification: The article is primarily about post-Assad Syria, rebel rule, and the political-security future of the Syrian state, which fits security/foreign policy most closely.

headline_no: Åpner Kiwi-butikk nummer syv i samme område i Oslo
headline_en: Opens Kiwi store number seven in the same area of Oslo
lead_en: Residents react to yet another Kiwi opening in the Carl Berner area, while an economist says the chain can afford to let nearby stores compete with each other to block rivals and keep sales within the group.
justification: The article is mainly about retail competition, market strategy, and supermarket concentration, which fits the economy category best.

headline_no: Eide fortsetter som utenriksminister
headline_en: Eide will continue as foreign minister
lead_en: As Jonas Gahr Støre finalizes a new Labour-only government after the Centre Party’s exit, Espen Barth Eide is set to remain foreign minister.
justification: The article is primarily about cabinet formation and government continuity, with foreign policy as a secondary dimension because Eide is retained amid an unstable international situation.

headline_no: DNB: Kraftig økning av økonomisk kriminalitet
headline_en: DNB: Sharp increase in financial crime
lead_en: DNB reports a strong rise in fraud attempts, money laundering, and terror financing, warning that organized criminal networks are increasingly targeting Norway’s financial system.
justification: The article is mainly about financial crime, fraud, and money laundering in Norway, which falls outside the main election issue categories, though it has a clear economic dimension.

headline_no: – Dette blir veldig kaotisk
headline_en: “This is going to be very chaotic”
lead_en: Trump’s trade war has begun, and analysts warn that tariffs on major U.S. trading partners could quickly create economic turmoil that may also affect Norway.
justification: The article is primarily about tariffs, trade war, prices, financial markets, and possible effects on the Norwegian economy, which fits the economy category best.

headline_no: Amerikanerne er ikke de eneste som vil sette seg selv først
headline_en: Americans are not the only ones who want to put themselves first
lead_en: The commentary argues that Trump’s tariff threats are testing how far U.S. power can go, while warning that aggressive trade tactics may push allies and partners away.
justification: The article is primarily about tariffs, trade conflict, and economic pressure between the United States and its partners, with superpower strategy as a secondary security dimension.

headline_no: Mer enn 1000 kr i måneden å spare på å handle her
headline_en: More than NOK 1,000 a month can be saved by shopping here
lead_en: The article finds that families can save substantial amounts on fruit and vegetables by shopping at immigrant-run produce stores rather than major supermarket chains.
justification: The article is primarily about food prices, household budgets, and how to reduce grocery costs, with healthy eating as a secondary aspect.

headline_no: Prisbyks hos Obos i Oslo i januar
headline_en: Price jump at OBOS in Oslo in January
lead_en: Prices for used OBOS homes in Oslo rose sharply in January, surprising the housing cooperative’s chief economist and suggesting a very strong housing market at the start of 2025.
justification: The article is primarily about housing prices, interest rates, purchasing conditions, and the broader household economy, which fits the economy/cost-of-living category.

headline_no: Kommunen skal rydde opp i P-plass-skilting
headline_en: The municipality will clean up the parking-space signage
lead_en: Oslo’s city government says it will fix confusing parking signage after disabled drivers were fined on spaces they believed were valid HC parking spots.
justification: The article is mainly about municipal governance, administrative competence, and public trust in local rules, with accessibility for disabled drivers as a secondary aspect.

headline_no: Spenning bak vennskapet
headline_en: Tension behind the friendship
lead_en: The commentary argues that Trump wants a Saudi-Israeli normalization deal, but Netanyahu may resist the concessions needed to sustain a ceasefire and address the Palestinian issue.
justification: The article is primarily about Gaza, the ceasefire, and the Palestinian question within Trump’s regional diplomacy, with wider Middle East security as a secondary issue.

headline_no: Tror kvinnen døde flere dager før mannen
headline_en: Police believe the woman died several days before the man
lead_en: Police say a 19-year-old woman found dead in Bergen had no visible injuries, and investigators now believe she died several days before the 20-year-old man found with her.
justification: The article is a crime and police investigation report about two deaths in Bergen, which does not fit the main election issue categories.

headline_no: Nå er det uvanlige folks tur
headline_en: Now it is unusual people’s turn
lead_en: The editorial says Labour has improved its chances by forming a pure Ap government and bringing back Jens Stoltenberg, but argues it must now show a more forward-looking and modern social-democratic agenda.
justification: The article is primarily about government change, Labour’s leadership, and electoral strategy, while also touching on economic and policy direction.

headline_no: Andreas Slettholm: Mange voldtekter blir hverken anmeldt eller ender med dom. Det er ikke noe godt argument for å straffe for strengt.
headline_en: Andreas Slettholm: Many rapes are neither reported nor end in conviction. That is not a good argument for punishing too harshly.
lead_en: The commentator argues that although many rape cases never lead to conviction, this is not a reason to weaken legal safeguards or impose overly harsh sentences in cases with unclear evidence.
justification: The article is primarily about legal standards, sentencing, and rule-of-law concerns in rape cases, which fits governance and justice rather than a specific policy domain.

headline_no: Sprengte konsulentavtale med 130 millioner
headline_en: Exceeded consultant contract by 130 million
lead_en: Norway’s ministries exceeded a framework agreement for ICT consultants by nearly NOK 130 million, and continued using consultants even after realizing the contract limit had been passed.
justification: The article is mainly about public administration, procurement failures, and weak state control over spending and consultants, which fits governance and competence.

headline_no: – Jeg hadde ikke planlagt å vaere her. Jeg hadde andre planer for livet mitt.
headline_en: “I had not planned to be here. I had other plans for my life.”
lead_en: Jens Stoltenberg returns to Norwegian politics as finance minister, saying he had not planned it but felt duty-bound to say yes when Jonas Gahr Støre asked him to serve.
justification: The article is primarily about cabinet formation, political leadership, and Stoltenberg’s return to government, with finance policy as a secondary aspect.

headline_no: En slik finansminister har Norge aldri før hatt
headline_en: Norway has never had a finance minister like this before
lead_en: The commentary says Labour has been transformed in a matter of days, with Jens Stoltenberg’s return as finance minister giving the party new momentum and potentially reshaping the election campaign.
