The article centers on Norway’s review of investments linked to the war in Gaza and possible violations of international law.
justification: The article is primarily about the Oil Fund’s Israel-related investments and the Gaza war, with a secondary governance dimension concerning procedures and political oversight by the finance minister.

headline_no: Kirkevandringen
headline_en: The church walk
lead_en: The article describes the extraordinary relocation of Kiruna’s historic wooden church in Sweden after mining made the ground under the old town unsafe. The move is presented as a major logistical and cultural event for the whole city.
justification: The article is primarily a human-interest and infrastructure story about relocating a church in Sweden, with a secondary environmental dimension because mining and unstable ground triggered the move.

headline_no: Norge tettere på Ukrainas våpenindustri. – Et livsviktig samarbeid.
headline_en: Norway moves closer to Ukraine’s arms industry. “A vital partnership.”
lead_en: Norway is increasing support for Ukraine’s defense industry by funding local production, including a Norwegian-developed protective breathing mask to be made in Ukraine. The article presents this as a way to save lives now while strengthening Ukraine’s long-term military capacity.
justification: The article is primarily about defense cooperation, military support for Ukraine, and Norway’s role in European security, framed positively through the government’s new strategy.

headline_no: Politiet ville fjerne sultestreikende aktivist
headline_en: Police wanted to remove hunger-striking activist
lead_en: A climate activist who has been hunger-striking outside the Norwegian parliament for nearly three weeks had his tent removed by police. The protest targets Norwegian oil policy and is described as a peaceful climate demonstration.
justification: The article is primarily about climate activism and opposition to Norwegian oil policy, with a secondary governance aspect related to police handling of the protest and criticism of the government.

headline_no: Hvordan endte hun opp alene og drept?
headline_en: How did she end up alone and killed?
lead_en: After the murder at Kampen in Oslo, urgent questions are being raised about why a care worker was alone on a night shift and whether the police shared enough information about the perpetrator’s radicalization.
justification: The article is primarily about safety failures in frontline welfare and social care services, with a secondary link to extremist radicalization and security concerns.

headline_no: Arbeidende kapital. Et ordspill i mesterklasse.
headline_en: Working capital. A masterclass in wordplay.
lead_en: The commentator examines how the phrase “working capital” has become a powerful rhetorical tool in the election campaign to argue for cutting wealth tax on shares and business assets.
justification: The article primarily discusses the wealth tax debate and redistribution, while secondarily touching on election strategy and possible cross-party tax negotiations.

headline_no: Familien sørger over Tamima Nibras Juhar (34). 18-åring er siktet for drap og terror.
headline_en: The family mourns Tamima Nibras Juhar (34). An 18-year-old is charged with murder and terrorism.
lead_en: The family of Tamima Nibras Juhar says they are in deep grief after she was killed at a supported housing facility in Oslo, where an 18-year-old man has been charged with murder and terrorism.
justification: The article is primarily about a terror-classified killing linked to right-wing extremism, with a secondary connection to safety in welfare and care services.

headline_no: Kjent for PST, siktet for terror
headline_en: Known to the PST, charged with terrorism
lead_en: An 18-year-old already known to Norway’s security service has now also been charged with terrorism after the killing of Tamima Nibras Juhar in Oslo, where police say the act was politically motivated and intended to spread fear.
justification: The article is primarily about terrorism, radicalization, and the role of the security services, with a secondary focus on safety failures in a child welfare-related housing service.

headline_no: – Vi er langt unna en fredsavtale
headline_en: We are far from a peace agreement
lead_en: As Russian attacks on Ukraine intensify, Jonas Gahr Støre visited Kyiv and pledged that Norway will provide NOK 85 billion in support in 2026 as well, while warning that a peace deal remains far away.
justification: The article is primarily about Norway’s foreign policy, security support, and the war in Ukraine, while secondarily touching on domestic political positioning around the government’s support package.

headline_no: Ingenting ved dette er normalt
headline_en: Nothing about this is normal
lead_en: In Kyiv, the article uses the destruction of a residential building and Støre’s visit to underline how Russia’s war against Ukraine has transformed European security and will shape Norwegian politics for years to come.
justification: The article is primarily about war, European security, and Norway’s role in supporting Ukraine, with a secondary emphasis on how these developments affect Norwegian political leadership and priorities.

headline_no: Byrådet innfører anonym retting
headline_en: The city government introduces anonymous grading
lead_en: Oslo’s city government will introduce anonymous grading on selected school tests after a pilot project, arguing it can reduce bias and make assessment fairer, while teachers’ representatives warn it will create more work.
justification: The article is primarily about schools and public services, focusing on grading practices in Oslo schools, with a secondary governance angle about a political reform introduced by the city government.

headline_no: Fire journalister blant 15 drepte i sykehusangrep
headline_en: Four journalists among 15 killed in hospital attack
lead_en: Four journalists, including photojournalist Mariam Dagga, were among 15 people killed in two Israeli attacks on Nasser Hospital in Gaza, where they had been documenting the hunger crisis and rescue efforts.
justification: The article is primarily about the Gaza war and attacks connected to Israel/Palestine, with a secondary foreign-policy dimension through Norway’s response to the killing of journalists.

headline_no: Dette må du vite om formuesskatten
headline_en: What you need to know about the wealth tax
lead_en: The article explains why Norway’s wealth tax has become one of the fiercest issues in the election campaign, even though only a minority pay it and it accounts for a relatively small share of total state revenue.
justification: The article is primarily an explainer on the wealth tax, its effects, and the political controversy around redistribution and business taxation, with a secondary link to broader economic competitiveness.

headline_no: Merkel angrer ikke på at hun ønsket flyktninger velkommen
headline_en: Merkel does not regret welcoming refugees
lead_en: Ten years after saying “We can do this,” Angela Merkel says she does not regret opening Germany’s borders to Syrian refugees, even though she acknowledges that the move helped strengthen the far right.
justification: The article is primarily about refugee policy and immigration, with a secondary focus on political leadership and the electoral consequences for Merkel and the German right.

headline_no: Aktor ber om 120 dagers ubetinget fengsel for tiltalt spanjol
headline_en: Prosecutor seeks 120 days of unconditional prison for accused Spaniard
lead_en: A prosecutor is seeking a 120-day prison sentence for a Spanish man accused of assaulting an assistant referee at Norway Cup, after a violent incident in which the referee suffered head injuries.
justification: The article is primarily a crime and court report about a violent incident at a football tournament, which does not fit the main election issue categories.

headline_no: Ekspert: Matkjedene har ingenting å frykte
headline_en: Expert: The grocery chains have nothing to fear
lead_en: After sharp food price increases, Industry Minister Cecilie Myrseth met with grocery sector actors, but critics say the government is showing little real willingness or ability to bring prices down.
justification: The article is primarily about rising food prices and cost-of-living pressures, while secondarily criticizing the government’s handling of competition and market regulation.

headline_no: Oslos trikker har fått feil type sand – alle må på verksted
headline_en: Oslo’s trams have been given the wrong type of sand – all must go to the workshop
lead_en: Oslo’s trams are running extra cautiously after being filled with overly coarse sand in the traction system, and reduced service will be introduced while every tram is sent in to have it replaced.
justification: The article is primarily about a disruption in public transport service, which falls under public services rather than a broader political or economic policy debate.

headline_no: Ketamin er blitt godkjent som offentlig behandling for alvorlig depresjon
headline_en: Ketamine has been approved as a public treatment for severe depression
lead_en: Norway will now offer ketamine treatment in the public health system for severe, treatment-resistant depression, giving a new option to patients who have not improved with existing therapies.
justification: The article is primarily about healthcare policy and access to a new publicly funded treatment for severe depression.

headline_no: Elever må laere at livet er fullt av ubehag
headline_en: Students must learn that life is full of discomfort
lead_en: It can be uncomfortable to give presentations, take part in gym class, or make new friends, but schools should help students face such challenges rather than avoid them. The editorial argues that shielding anxious pupils from discomfort leaves them less prepared for adult life.
justification: The article is primarily about schools, student well-being, and how public institutions should handle anxiety and social development, which fits health/welfare/public services best.

headline_no: Diskusjonen om Nationaltheatrets fremtid er neppe over
headline_en: The discussion about the National Theatre’s future is probably not over
lead_en: The columnist argues that Culture Minister Lubna Jaffery’s attempt to settle the future of the decaying National Theatre may not hold. A competing proposal to expand and renovate the historic building in central Oslo could reopen the political debate.
justification: The article is mainly about a major public cultural institution and state provision of services, while secondarily criticizing Labour-led handling and political decision-making around the project.

headline_no: Reguleringsrekord i bystyret: Vil vedta 2300 nye boliger i ett møte.
headline_en: Record zoning decision in the city council: Wants to approve 2,300 new homes in a single meeting.
lead_en: Oslo is set to approve zoning for around 2,300 homes in one city council meeting, more than were approved in all of last year. The article frames faster housing approvals as part of a broader push to boost construction and ease pressure in the housing market.
justification: The article is primarily about housing supply, market pressure, and efforts to speed up construction, which fits the economy/cost-of-living category best, with a secondary public-services planning angle.

headline_no: Tre års byggeforbud i Oslo: – Nå går det ikke lenger
headline_en: Three years of a building ban in Oslo: “This cannot go on”
lead_en: For more than three years, 28,000 properties in Oslo have faced a full building ban while national authorities object to the city’s new small-house zoning plan over flood and landslide risk. Local politicians say the delay is untenable and is worsening Oslo’s housing shortage.
justification: The article is mainly about housing supply and regulatory delays affecting construction in Oslo, with a secondary climate/environment dimension tied to flood and landslide risk assessments.

headline_no: Venstre viser vinglestyrke
headline_en: Venstre shows the strength of wavering
lead_en: Venstre leader Guri Melby says the party could let Jonas Gahr Støre remain prime minister if a right-wing government does not deliver much stronger climate policy. The commentary presents Venstre as a possible kingmaker and focuses on coalition dynamics ahead of the election.
justification: The article is primarily about government formation, coalition bargaining, and election strategy, with climate policy serving as the key secondary issue.

headline_no: – Ubegripelig at ikke sikkerheten hennes ble ivaretatt
headline_en: “Incomprehensible that her safety was not safeguarded”
lead_en: Child welfare workers are reacting strongly after a 34-year-old employee was left alone with an 18-year-old known for far-right extremist views and was later killed on the job. The article focuses on failures in workplace safety, risk assessment, and employer responsibility in the child welfare system.
justification: The article is primarily about safety, staffing, and employer responsibility in child welfare services, with a secondary dimension involving racism and far-right extremism.

headline_no: 15 år og fem høyreekstreme drap: – Faerre, men verre
headline_en: 15 years and five far-right murders: “Fewer, but worse”
lead_en: After the killing of Tamima Nibras Juhar, the article reviews more than 20 far-right attacks in Norway since 2010 and highlights a shift toward fewer but more severe acts of extremist violence. Researchers say online radicalization and lone-actor terrorism have become more prominent.
justification: The article is primarily about terrorism, extremist violence, and public security in Norway, with a secondary link to anti-immigrant targeting and far-right ideology.

headline_no: «Norge sto opp mot terroren. Men hatet forsvant aldri.»
headline_en: “Norway stood up to terrorism. But the hatred never disappeared.”
lead_en: Commentator Daniel Røed-Johansen argues that Tamima Nibras Juhar was killed because she was an immigrant and a Muslim, and that the attack has shaken Norway’s sense of trust and safety. He places the killing in a broader context of far-right extremism, racism, and anti-Muslim hatred.
justification: The article is primarily about terrorism, extremist violence, and societal security, with a secondary focus on anti-immigrant and anti-Muslim hatred.

headline_no: Politiprofessor: Gamle politiattester gjør at overgripere kan gå under radaren
headline_en: Police professor: Old background checks allow abusers to slip under the radar
lead_en: A law professor is calling for a full review after Aftenposten revealed that a convicted child rapist was able to remain in a job because employers cannot routinely renew criminal record checks for existing staff. The article argues that the current system gives employers false reassurance and may leave children inadequately protected.
justification: The article is mainly about shortcomings in laws, oversight, and administrative rules on police certificates, with a secondary connection to safeguarding in welfare and public-service settings.

headline_no: Fersk meningsmåling: Frp går tilbake, Ap vokser mest.
headline_en: New poll: The Progress Party falls back, Labour grows the most.
lead_en: A new poll less than two weeks before the election shows Frp losing support while Høyre gains voters back, but Labour posts the strongest growth overall. The article focuses on shifting party support and the implications for government formation.
justification: The article is primarily about polling, party competition, and possible parliamentary majorities, which fits governance/leadership rather than any specific policy issue.

headline_no: Slik kan mobilisering avgjøre valget
headline_en: How mobilization can decide the election
lead_en: The article argues that the election may be decided by voter loyalty and turnout, as former supporters return to Labour and Høyre while less reliable protest voters drift away from Frp. It focuses on how these shifts could determine parliamentary majorities and whether Jonas Gahr Støre stays in power.
justification: The article is mainly about polling, voter mobilization, and government formation, and it presents Labour’s renewed voter loyalty and chances of staying in power in a favourable light.

headline_no: Kan disse ha haiket i feriekofferten din? Da haster det.
headline_en: Could these have hitchhiked in your vacation suitcase? Then it is urgent.
lead_en: The article warns that bedbugs may be brought home from travel and stresses that suspected infestations should be dealt with quickly before they spread. A pest researcher explains how to spot them and why early action can reduce harm and costs.
justification: The article is primarily about public health, household well-being, and pest control advice, which fits health/welfare/public services best.

headline_no: Venstre-representant vil kaste Venstre-byråd
headline_en: Venstre representative wants to oust Venstre city councillor
lead_en: A Venstre representative in Oslo is calling for the party’s own social affairs councillor, Julianne Ferskaug, to be replaced, arguing that she has not been visible enough in eastern Oslo and multicultural communities. The article centers on internal party conflict and local political leadership.
justification: The article is primarily about internal party conflict, political leadership, and local governance rather than a specific policy domain.

headline_no: Russland forsøkte i 37 år. Trump fikk til en fredsavtale på rekordtid.
headline_en: Russia tried for 37 years. Trump secured a peace deal in record time.
lead_en: The article examines the peace agreement between Armenia and Azerbaijan brokered by Donald Trump and the planned new transport corridor through Armenia. It argues that the deal could weaken Russia’s grip on the Caucasus while also reducing Iran’s regional leverage.
justification: The article is primarily about foreign policy, regional power shifts, and security dynamics involving Russia, Armenia, Azerbaijan, Iran, and the United States.

headline_no: Høiby kan straffes med inntil 16 års fengsel
headline_en: Høiby could be sentenced to up to 16 years in prison
lead_en: The article explains that although prosecutors said the maximum sentence in Marius Borg Høiby’s case is ten years, the court could in theory impose up to 16 years if he is convicted on multiple counts. It focuses on how sentencing rules may apply in his upcoming trial.
justification: The article is primarily a legal/crime report about sentencing in a criminal case, which does not fit the main election issue categories, though it has a secondary governance/legal-system angle.

headline_no: Kappet fem kabler med ankeret. Kapteinen hevder det var et uhell.
headline_en: Cut five cables with the anchor. The captain claims it was an accident.
lead_en: Prosecutors say an oil tanker dragged its anchor across the Gulf of Finland for hours and cut one power cable and four data cables, while the captain claims it was an accident caused by a technical fault. The case centers on possible deliberate sabotage of critical infrastructure.
justification: The article is primarily about damage to critical undersea infrastructure, cross-border security, and a criminal case with national-security implications.

headline_no: Økonomisk kortslutning, politisk korttenkt
headline_en: Economic short circuit, politically short-sighted
lead_en: The government is increasing support for household energy-saving measures through Enova, while at the same time planning to spend far more subsidizing electricity consumption through the “Norgespris” scheme. The editorial argues that this is economically self-defeating and politically short-sighted.
justification: The article is primarily about electricity prices, subsidies, and incentives for household energy use, framed as criticism of the government’s economic policy, with a secondary climate/energy-efficiency dimension.

headline_no: Harald Eia provoserer om kvinnehelse og har vandret ut i et minefelt
headline_en: Harald Eia provokes on women’s health and has wandered into a minefield
lead_en: Commentator Rakel Haugen Strand criticizes Harald Eia for suggesting that women’s strong interest in health can make them sicker. She argues that women often seek medical help more because their symptoms are dismissed and women’s health has long been neglected.
justification: The article is primarily about women’s health, healthcare recognition, diagnosis, and medical stigma rather than party politics or election strategy.

headline_no: Forlot møte som skulle handle om våpenhvile, for å feire med bosettere
headline_en: Left a meeting that was supposed to be about a ceasefire to celebrate with settlers
lead_en: Hope briefly rose for a new Gaza ceasefire after Hamas accepted a proposal for a 60-day pause, but Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu instead escalated the war and faced growing criticism inside Israel. The article highlights his choice to leave a cabinet meeting early to attend a celebration with West Bank settlers.
justification: The article is primarily about the Gaza war and ceasefire politics, with a secondary foreign-policy/security dimension related to Israel’s military strategy and regional diplomacy.

headline_no: Hungersnød blant barn i Gaza: Dette skjer når kroppen sulter
headline_en: Famine among children in Gaza: What happens when the body starves
lead_en: The UN estimates that 132,000 children under five in Gaza will suffer acute malnutrition by June 2026 because of Israel’s aid blockade. The article explains how starvation affects children’s bodies and why they are far more vulnerable than adults.
justification: The article is primarily about the Gaza humanitarian crisis and Israel’s blockade, with a secondary health angle focused on child malnutrition and medical consequences.

headline_no: Norgesgruppen tjente nesten 2 mrd. før skatt første halvår
headline_en: Norgesgruppen earned nearly NOK 2 billion before tax in the first half of the year
lead_en: Norgesgruppen reported NOK 60.4 billion in revenue and NOK 1.965 billion in pre-tax profit in the first six months of the year. The article links the results to rising grocery prices and the political debate over the power of supermarket chains during the election campaign.
justification: The article is primarily about rising food prices, household economic pressure, and supermarket profits, with a secondary governance angle through campaign debate and the government’s meetings with grocery chains.

headline_no: Skyhøye dødstall i krigen, men i Moskva er det full fest
headline_en: Sky-high death tolls in the war, but in Moscow the party goes on
lead_en: Despite massive Russian battlefield losses in the war against Ukraine, daily life in Moscow remains festive and largely detached from the conflict. The article argues that Putin has insulated the big cities by relying on recruits from poorer regions and paying heavily for enlistment.
justification: The article is primarily about Russia’s war against Ukraine, military losses, and Putin’s wartime strategy, which squarely fits security/foreign policy.

headline_no: Eierne advarte ansatte om formuesskatt. Samtidig tok de ut gigantisk utbytte.
headline_en: The owners warned employees about the wealth tax. At the same time, they paid themselves a giant dividend.
lead_en: The owners of Oluf Lorentzen told employees that the wealth tax was draining the company and could threaten jobs. The article argues that most of the family’s wealth and dividend income comes from its large stake in Norgesgruppen, not from the delicatessen importer itself.
justification: The article is primarily about the wealth tax, private ownership, and dividend extraction, with a secondary governance/election angle because the owners explicitly tied the issue to the ongoing campaign.

headline_no: Trump styrer USAs økonomi mer og mer som en diktator
headline_en: Trump is running the U.S. economy more and more like a dictator
lead_en: The commentary argues that Donald Trump is tightening political control over the U.S. economy by pressuring the central bank, intervening in private companies, and expanding presidential influence over regulators. It presents “America First” as a model of personalized state control rather than market liberalism.
justification: The article is primarily about U.S. power, international economic statecraft, and the broader geopolitical implications of Trump’s rule, with a secondary governance dimension concerning executive control and institutional independence.

headline_no: Frp har et eget ansvar for å forebygge ekstremisme
headline_en: The Progress Party has its own responsibility to prevent extremism
lead_en: The commentary argues that while Frp is not responsible for terror, it has a particular ability and duty to help prevent right-wing radicalization among people in or near its political milieu. It says mainstream society is unlikely to reach such individuals, whereas Frp and its youth wing might.
justification: The article is primarily about party responsibility, democratic politics, and extremism prevention within Frp’s political sphere, with a secondary link to anti-immigration rhetoric associated with the far right.

headline_no: Støre smiler av eks-kollega Pollestads kaffe latte-stunt
headline_en: Støre laughs at ex-colleague Pollestad’s café latte stunt
lead_en: After criticism that Labour and the Centre Party neglected the cities while governing together, Jonas Gahr Støre and Kamzy Gunaratnam now promise a more city-friendly Labour government. The article contrasts Labour’s new urban messaging with the Centre Party’s anti-Oslo rhetoric and past coalition tensions.
justification: The article is primarily about Labour’s campaign positioning, coalition dynamics after the split with the Centre Party, and leadership messaging toward urban voters rather than a specific policy issue.

headline_no: Støre er klart mest populaer
headline_en: Støre is clearly the most popular
lead_en: A new poll shows Jonas Gahr Støre is the preferred prime minister among 46 percent of respondents, well ahead of Erna Solberg and Sylvi Listhaug. The article focuses on leader popularity and late-campaign positioning ahead of the parliamentary election.
justification: The article is primarily about polling, leadership popularity, and election competition over the prime ministership, which fits governance/leadership rather than a substantive policy issue.

headline_no: Syvdoblet valgkampstøtte til Frp
headline_en: Campaign support for the Progress Party has increased sevenfold
lead_en: The article reports that Frp has received seven times more campaign donations than before the previous parliamentary election, as money in Norwegian politics continues to surge. It highlights concerns that large and unequal political donations may undermine democratic equality and influence election outcomes.
justification: The article is primarily about campaign finance, political influence, and democratic fairness in the election, which fits governance, with a secondary link to the wealth-tax conflict driving some donor mobilization.

headline_no: Dyr strøm har gitt lønnsfest for sjefer i offentlig eide kraftselskaper
headline_en: Expensive electricity has created a pay bonanza for bosses in publicly owned power companies
lead_en: An investigation finds that top executives at Norway’s largest publicly owned power companies have received far bigger pay rises than ordinary workers in recent years. The article links this to soaring electricity prices and growing criticism of executive pay in companies owned by the public.
justification: The article is primarily about high electricity prices, energy-sector profits, and their economic effects, with a secondary governance angle concerning pay practices in publicly owned companies.

headline_no: Forsvarer: Spansk mann frikjent for Norway Cup-vold
headline_en: Defense lawyer: Spanish man acquitted in Norway Cup violence case
lead_en: A Spanish football supporter accused of assaulting an assistant referee at the Norway Cup has been acquitted on self-defense grounds. The court found that the referee had acted aggressively and that the situation unfolded too quickly for other options.
justification: The article is primarily a criminal court report about a violent incident at a football tournament in Norway and does not fit the main election-related issue categories.

headline_no: Formuesskatt er blitt årets store snakkis blant unge menn. – Jeg frykter at jeg må flytte.
headline_en: Wealth tax has become this year’s big talking point among young men. “I fear I may have to move.”
lead_en: The article describes how the wealth tax has become a major election issue among young men, even among those who do not pay it, driven partly by social media and podcasts. It highlights business owners who say the tax forces dividend payouts, weakens investment, and may push Norwegian ownership abroad.
justification: The article is primarily about the wealth tax and its alleged effects on business owners, investment, and ownership, while also framing Labour’s finance minister and national politicians as unsympathetic to these concerns.

headline_no: MDG-topp ga opp, nå vil han vaere med igjen
headline_en: Top MDG figure gave up, now he wants to rejoin
lead_en: Former Green Youth leader Teodor Bruu, who recently said he had given up the climate struggle, now says he wants to return to the campaign. He says the prospect of Sylvi Listhaug representing Norway internationally and the urgency of the climate crisis pulled him back in.
justification: The article is primarily about climate politics and renewed mobilization in MDG around the election, with a secondary governance element tied to campaign dynamics and party activism.

headline_no: Derfor kvittet Oljefondet seg med aksjer i amerikansk anleggsgigant
headline_en: Why the Oil Fund got rid of shares in an American construction giant
lead_en: The article explains why Norway’s sovereign wealth fund sold its Caterpillar shares after concluding that the company’s machinery was linked to serious violations in Gaza. It places the decision in a broader pattern of the fund divesting from controversial Israel-related holdings.
justification: The article is primarily about Gaza, Israel-linked war conduct, and Norway’s sovereign wealth fund divestment response, with a secondary foreign-policy and international-law dimension.

headline_no: Trynefaktoren må ut av skoler over hele Norge
headline_en: The “face factor” must be removed from schools across Norway
lead_en: Students in Oslo who tested anonymous grading say it feels fairer, and research suggests teachers’ perceptions can affect marks. The editorial argues anonymous grading should be expanded to make school assessment more equitable.
justification: The article is primarily about fairness in grading and the operation of the school system, which fits education and public services.

headline_no: India får ikke lov til å vaere nøytralt
headline_en: India is not allowed to remain neutral
lead_en: New U.S. tariffs on Indian goods are damaging relations between India and the United States, partly to pressure India over Russian energy imports. The commentary asks whether Narendra Modi will respond by moving closer to China or trying to preserve India’s balancing position.
justification: The article is primarily about geopolitics, great-power alignment, Russia’s war in Ukraine, and India’s foreign-policy positioning, with trade tariffs as a secondary economic dimension.

headline_no: Arkitekturopprøret slakter plan for Furuset
headline_en: The Architecture Uprising tears apart the plan for Furuset
lead_en: Landowners want to build a new town center at Furuset in Oslo, but Arkitekturopprøret harshly criticizes the design as soulless, formulaic development. The dispute centers on whether the plan will create a real urban environment or repeat past planning mistakes.
justification: The article is mainly about urban design and architectural planning for a local development project, which does not fit the main election issue categories, with a minor connection to local public space and community services.

headline_no: Fra standup til sykehjem
headline_en: From stand-up to a nursing home
lead_en: A stolen painting looted from a Jewish art dealer during World War II was seemingly revealed in an Argentine real-estate listing tied to a Nazi official’s family. But when police searched the house, the artwork had already been replaced by a painting of horses.
justification: The article is about Nazi-looted art, historical restitution, and an international police investigation, which does not fit the main election issue categories.

headline_no: Ekspert mener demokratene kan få en slutt på dette om de tør
headline_en: Expert says Democrats can put an end to this if they dare
lead_en: Armed National Guard soldiers are now patrolling Washington, D.C., and a former CIA analyst calls it a constitutional breakdown rather than a public-safety measure. He argues Trump is using troop deployments to intimidate Democratic leaders and voters.
justification: The article is primarily about democratic institutions, presidential power, and political control in the United States, with security forces as a secondary element.

headline_no: Nå angriper han Europa – igjen
headline_en: Now he is attacking Europe again
lead_en: Trump is threatening Europe with punitive tariffs if it continues regulating major U.S. tech firms through digital taxes and EU tech laws. The commentary argues these rules are meant to protect consumers and democracy, not discriminate against American companies.
justification: The article is primarily about transatlantic conflict, international pressure, and EU-US foreign-economic relations, with the trade and regulatory dispute as a secondary economic issue.

headline_no: Mange sivile drept i nattangrep
headline_en: Many civilians killed in nighttime attack
lead_en: At least 15 people, including four children, were killed and nearly 50 injured in a massive Russian drone and missile attack on Kyiv. The article describes widespread destruction and renewed international condemnation, including from Norway.
justification: The article is primarily about Russia’s war against Ukraine, civilian casualties, international reactions, and Norway’s response, which fits security and foreign policy.

headline_no: Folks samlede skattenivå skulle holdes uendret. Slik gikk det.
headline_en: People’s overall tax level was supposed to remain unchanged. Here is what happened.
lead_en: Labour promised before the 2021 election to keep the overall level of taxes and fees on people’s income unchanged, but the pledge depended on a later clarification excluding dividend tax. The article says the promise was kept only under Labour’s narrower definition, while critics argue it was broken.
justification: The article primarily concerns Labour’s tax pledge, dividend taxation, and whether the government increased the tax burden, with a secondary focus on political credibility and leadership.

headline_no: Anonyme kontoer på sosiale medier preger valgkampen
headline_en: Anonymous accounts on social media are shaping the election campaign
lead_en: Anonymous political accounts are gaining huge reach in Norway’s election campaign, while the money behind some of them remains unclear. The article warns that social-media algorithms and opaque funding may be reshaping how young voters encounter political information.
justification: The article is primarily about election campaigning, political influence, transparency, and democratic fairness rather than a specific substantive policy issue.

headline_no: Millionfest for kraftdirektører møtt med raseri fra politikere
headline_en: Million-party for power executives met with outrage from politicians
lead_en: Politicians react with disbelief after it emerged that executives in publicly owned power companies received unusually large pay increases during the electricity crisis. The article links the outrage to falling purchasing power and high power bills for ordinary people.
justification: The article is primarily about the electricity crisis, energy-sector profits, and the burden on households, with a secondary focus on political oversight of publicly owned companies.

headline_no: Politiet: Terrorsiktet 18-åring planla også angrep mot moské
headline_en: Police: Terror-charged 18-year-old also planned an attack on a mosque
lead_en: Police say an 18-year-old already charged in a politically motivated killing also stated that he intended to attack a mosque in Hønefoss. Investigators say his ability to carry out further attacks appears to have been limited, but the case is still under investigation.
justification: The article is primarily about terrorism, public security, and police investigation of a planned attack in Norway.

headline_no: Forsker: Stadig flere får alkohol av foreldrene til å ta med på fest
headline_en: Researcher: More and more young people are getting alcohol from their parents to bring to parties
lead_en: A new public-health survey shows that alcohol use among teenagers is rising again, and far more minors now report getting alcohol from their parents. Researchers say this more liberal parenting approach is linked to more frequent and riskier drinking.
justification: The article is primarily about youth alcohol use, public health, and parental behavior, which fits health and welfare issues.Tenåring har laget app som skal hjelpe folk som vil slutte med porno Den britiske gründeren sier han tjener millioner på appen som tilbyr KI-terapeut og panikkknapp. Norsk sexolog er kritisk til metodene. Aftenposten29 Aug 2025Ruben Grøndahl Utaker ruben@aftenposten.no Foto: Skjermdump / @alexsllater på Youtube Kompisene Alex Slater (venstre) og businesspartneren Connor McLaren (høyre) hevder at de tjener fett på kampen mot porno. Pornobransjen er en global milliardindustri. Nå prøver Alex Slater (19) å utfordre den.

headline_no: Mange bommer grovt på matvareprisen, viser ny studie
headline_en: Many people misjudge grocery prices badly, new study shows
lead_en: A new study finds that shoppers with better knowledge of food prices pay less overall, while many consumers guess prices very inaccurately. The article suggests that stronger price awareness can save households thousands of kroner a year.
justification: The article is primarily about grocery prices, household spending, and consumer purchasing power, which fits the cost-of-living and economy category.

headline_no: Tenåring har laget app som skal hjelpe folk som vil slutte med porno
headline_en: Teenager has made an app to help people who want to quit porn
lead_en: A British teen entrepreneur says his app helps users, many of them minors, stop watching porn through tools like an AI therapist and a panic button. A Norwegian sexologist criticizes the app for pathologizing normal sexual development and exploiting young men’s insecurity.
justification: The article is primarily about sexual health, youth well-being, and expert criticism of a mental-health style app, with a secondary cultural dimension that does not fit the main election issue categories.

headline_no: Nå popper de store edderkoppene frem
headline_en: Now the big spiders are popping up
lead_en: Large house spiders become especially visible in late summer and autumn, prompting many worried calls to Norway’s public health institute. Experts say this is mainly because male spiders are roaming in search of mates, and they are harmless to humans.
justification: The article is mainly a seasonal explanatory piece about spiders in Norwegian homes, which does not fit the main election issue categories, with a minor public-health information angle.

headline_no: Andelen personer uten videregående opplaering øker
headline_en: The share of people without upper secondary education is increasing
lead_en: New figures show that more 16–25-year-olds in Norway are neither completing nor on track to complete upper secondary education. The increase is linked partly to recent Ukrainian refugees, while completion rates in vocational education remain lower than in academic tracks.
justification: The article is primarily about education outcomes and public services for young people, with a secondary link to immigration through the impact of Ukrainian refugees on the statistics.

headline_no: Forskerens seks råd for å unngå demens
headline_en: The researcher’s six pieces of advice for avoiding dementia
lead_en: After more than 30 years of dementia research, Professor Tormod Fladby says there is still no cure, but lifestyle choices can reduce risk. He highlights exercise, social activity, diet, and overall well-being as key protective factors.
justification: The article is primarily about dementia prevention, health research, and lifestyle advice, which fits the health and welfare category.

headline_no: Hun slet med smerter. – Alt føltes mørkt og håpløst.
headline_en: She struggled with pain. “Everything felt dark and hopeless.”
lead_en: Annette Løno says chronic migraine and severe pain left her nearly bedridden before she began exploring alternative approaches centered on the vagus nerve. The article presents her recovery story alongside more cautious medical views on what is and is not well documented.
justification: The article is primarily about chronic pain, migraine, treatment approaches, and medical expert discussion, which fits the health and welfare category.

headline_no: Den norske kirke bør få mer enn andre
headline_en: The Church of Norway should receive more than others
lead_en: The Church of Norway is losing members while some other faith and belief communities are growing, creating funding problems under the current support system. An expert committee has proposed changes so the church’s special public responsibilities can be funded separately.
justification: The article is primarily about government rules and institutional design for public funding of religious communities, rather than a specific policy area in the coding scheme.

headline_no: Sjokk i Danmark. Norge later som ingenting.
headline_en: Shock in Denmark. Norway pretends nothing is happening.
lead_en: Denmark has been jolted by actions linked to Donald Trump, including moves against a Danish energy project and alleged influence operations in Greenland. The article argues that Norway is failing to confront what this means for its own security and foreign policy.
justification: The article is mainly about Norway’s security environment, the Trump-led United States, Europe, and the need for a clearer Norwegian foreign and defence strategy, while also criticizing the governing political leadership for not addressing it.

headline_no: Mener Norge ville vaert flere tusen milliarder fattigere hvis Frp hadde fått viljen sin
headline_en: Says Norway would have been several thousand billion poorer if the Progress Party had gotten its way
lead_en: Jens Stoltenberg argues that key political decisions made Norway’s sovereign wealth fund large, and that the Progress Party opposed several of them. He says Norway would have had far less saved if Frp had prevailed on oil taxation, equity allocation, and fiscal policy.
justification: The article centers on fiscal choices about oil revenues, taxation, and use of the sovereign wealth fund, while presenting Stoltenberg and Labour as responsible stewards against Frp’s alternative.

headline_no: Skal ha meldt seg inn i høyreekstrem gruppe før drapet
headline_en: Reportedly joined a far-right group before the killing
lead_en: An 18-year-old terror suspect who had long been seen as integrated in his local community was on PST’s radar over suspected far-right views before the fatal stabbing of a care worker in Oslo. Investigators say he also intended to attack a mosque.
justification: The article is primarily about politically motivated violence, extremism, and a killing targeting Muslims, which fits best under crime-related coverage, with a secondary governance angle because it discusses PST and institutional handling of the threat.

headline_no: Skal ha meldt seg inn i høyreekstrem gruppe før drapet
headline_en: Reportedly joined a far-right group before the killing
lead_en: An 18-year-old terror suspect had been on the police security service’s radar for suspected far-right views before he allegedly killed a care worker in Oslo. Investigators say he also intended to attack a mosque, raising questions about extremism and threat follow-up.
justification: The article is primarily about far-right anti-Muslim extremism, politically motivated violence, and a planned mosque attack, with a secondary governance aspect related to PST and institutional response.

headline_no: Gir demokratiet 50 prosent sjanse til å overleve Trump
headline_en: Gives democracy a 50 percent chance of surviving Trump
lead_en: A Norwegian political science professor says U.S. democracy now has only a fifty-fifty chance of surviving Donald Trump. The article argues that Trump has rapidly concentrated power and pushed the United States in a more authoritarian direction.
justification: The article is primarily about the international security and foreign-policy implications of democratic backsliding in the United States, with a secondary governance dimension focused on executive power and democratic institutions.

headline_no: Trump vil ha viljen sin. Truer med nye tollmurer.
headline_en: Trump wants his way. Threatens new tariff walls.
lead_en: Donald Trump is threatening higher tariffs and other penalties to pressure countries into dropping digital regulations and climate goals. The article describes how the U.S. is using trade power to defend tech companies and push other countries toward more fossil fuel use.
justification: The article is primarily about U.S. foreign economic coercion and international power politics, with a secondary focus on climate policy because Trump is also pressuring other countries to abandon green targets.

headline_no: Mens Trump splitter Vesten, samles resten i Kina
headline_en: As Trump splits the West, the rest gather in China
lead_en: China is hosting a major summit and military parade with leaders including Xi Jinping, Vladimir Putin, and Narendra Modi, showcasing a shifting global order without the United States. The article argues that Trump’s policies are pushing more countries closer to China and weakening the West’s position.
justification: The article is chiefly about global power shifts, great-power relations, and the strategic consequences of Trump’s foreign policy for the international order.

headline_no: Solberg avviser at Listhaug kan bli statsminister
headline_en: Solberg rejects the idea that Listhaug could become prime minister
lead_en: Erna Solberg says that, given the current polling, a centre-right majority would not result in Sylvi Listhaug becoming prime minister. She argues that the only realistic alternative to the current government is a Høyre-led coalition.
justification: The article is about coalition arithmetic, prime ministerial leadership, and government formation on the centre-right, which fits the governance and leadership category.

headline_no: Besk borgerlig baluba
headline_en: Bitter bourgeois turmoil
lead_en: Open conflict has broken out between Høyre and the Progress Party just over a week before the parliamentary election. The article argues that the dispute over whether Erna Solberg or Sylvi Listhaug should lead a potential right-wing government could cost the bloc victory.
justification: The article is about leadership conflict, coalition dynamics, and election strategy on the centre-right, with no primary substantive policy issue beyond government formation.

headline_no: – I Norge har vi skatt på ambisjoner. Det kan vi ikke ha.
headline_en: “In Norway, we tax ambition. We cannot have that.”
lead_en: Young entrepreneurs say they would prefer to build their companies in Norway, but are considering moving abroad because wealth tax and exit tax make it harder to raise capital and scale up. The article presents these tax rules as a barrier to entrepreneurship and future value creation.
justification: The article is primarily about the wealth tax and exit tax as obstacles to entrepreneurship and investment, and it frames the current Labour-led policy line as harmful to Norwegian business growth.

headline_no: Du er ikke liberal og kul, du er bare teit
headline_en: You are not liberal and cool, you are just stupid
lead_en: A growing share of 15- and 16-year-olds in Norway are getting alcohol from their parents before parties. The article argues that this does not reduce harm, but instead is linked to earlier and riskier drinking.
justification: The article is primarily about public health and youth alcohol consumption, focusing on parental behavior and health risks rather than party politics.

headline_no: Militaere bevegelser viser ny russisk test av «flyvende Tsjornobyl» i nord
headline_en: Military movements show a new Russian test of the “flying Chernobyl” in the north
lead_en: Norway’s intelligence service confirms that Russia is testing the nuclear-powered Burevestnik cruise missile in the High North. Experts warn that a failed test could cause radioactive contamination that affects Norwegian areas.
justification: The article is primarily about Russian weapons testing, intelligence, and security risks in Norway’s northern neighbourhood, with a secondary environmental angle because of the danger of radioactive pollution.

headline_no: Russlands gråsonekrig mot Europa fortsetter. I fjor tok det helt av.
headline_en: Russia’s gray-zone war against Europe continues. Last year it surged dramatically.
lead_en: Suspected Russian attacks on critical infrastructure in Europe rose sharply in 2024, and Norway was among the countries affected. The article says Europe is struggling to deter this sabotage, cyber activity, and other forms of hybrid warfare.
justification: The article is primarily about Russian hybrid warfare, sabotage, and threats to critical infrastructure in Europe and Norway, with a secondary governance angle because it discusses state preparedness and institutional response.

headline_no: Dette skjedde da Israel angrep et «Hamas-kamera»
headline_en: This is what happened when Israel attacked a “Hamas camera”
lead_en: Video analysis indicates that Israel struck the same location at Nasser Hospital in southern Gaza three times. The latter two strikes hit as rescuers and journalists were trying to help victims of the first attack, raising questions about Israel’s explanation.
justification: The article is primarily about the Gaza war and an Israeli strike on a hospital site, fitting the Gaza/Palestine category rather than Norwegian domestic politics.

headline_no: Israel erklaerer Gaza by som kampsone, stenger ute nødhjelp
headline_en: Israel declares Gaza City a combat zone, shuts out aid
lead_en: Israel says the tactical pause around Gaza City is over and that no more aid will be allowed in, as military operations intensify. The article also highlights strong international criticism, including from Norway’s foreign minister, of the offensive and forced displacement of Palestinians.
justification: The article is primarily about the Gaza war, humanitarian access, and international reactions to Israel’s offensive, with a secondary foreign-policy dimension because Norway joins a joint ministerial condemnation.

headline_no: LO må trekke seg ut av Palestina etter vedtak om Israel-boikott
headline_en: LO must withdraw from Palestine after adopting an Israel boycott
lead_en: After LO voted to support an economic boycott of Israel, the organization says it must pull out of its programs in Palestine because the decision has increased risks for staff and operations. The article highlights how a boycott intended to support Palestinians may instead undermine aid efforts on the ground.
justification: The article is primarily about the Israel–Palestine conflict and a Norwegian boycott decision’s consequences for Palestinian solidarity work, with a secondary governance angle because it discusses LO’s internal decision-making and the government’s response.

headline_no: Ber om fengsel for norsk sikkerhetsvakt som delte informasjon med Russland og Iran
headline_en: Prosecutors seek prison for Norwegian security guard who shared information with Russia and Iran
lead_en: Prosecutors are asking for more than six years in prison for a former security guard at the U.S. embassy in Oslo who admitted sharing sensitive information with Russia and Iran. The case centers on alleged espionage, security routines, and the potential harm of leaking names and internal details.
justification: The article is primarily about espionage, foreign intelligence threats, and national security, with a secondary governance aspect because it focuses on prosecution and legal handling of the case.

headline_no: Har helsekøene blitt kortere?
headline_en: Have the healthcare queues become shorter?
lead_en: The government says waiting times in the health system have fallen in 2025 after rising sharply in previous years. The article finds that queues have indeed become shorter, but mainly because hospitals used extraordinary funding and overtime, raising doubts about whether the improvement will last.
justification: The article is primarily about healthcare waiting times and hospital capacity, with a secondary governance angle because it evaluates whether the government has actually fulfilled its promise.

headline_no: Eviny-topp fikk nesten én million for å bli i selskapet
headline_en: Eviny executive received nearly one million to stay with the company
lead_en: A top Eviny executive received a loyalty bonus of nearly NOK 923,000 simply for remaining in her job, on top of her regular salary. The article raises questions about transparency and governance in a fully publicly owned power company.
justification: The article is mainly about governance, oversight, and executive accountability in a publicly owned company, rather than a substantive policy issue like taxes, health, or climate.

headline_no: Svaert mange boliger til salgs i Oslo: – Prisbremsen er på
headline_en: Very many homes for sale in Oslo: “The price brake is on”
lead_en: A large number of homes are on the market in Oslo, and more are remaining unsold, giving buyers more choice and reducing price pressure. Industry figures now expect weaker price growth and possibly falling prices in the capital.
justification: The article is primarily about housing market conditions, prices, interest rates, and affordability, which fit the economy and cost-of-living category.

headline_no: Hun falt for MDGs Støre-reklame
headline_en: She fell for MDG’s Støre advertisement
lead_en: The Green Party is trying to ride Jonas Gahr Støre’s momentum by telling voters that an MDG vote can help keep him in power. The article suggests this strategy may push MDG above the electoral threshold and make them important for a red-green majority.
justification: The article is mainly about election strategy, coalition arithmetic, and the threshold battle, while secondarily touching on climate because MDG frames itself as the party that can make a Støre government greener.

headline_no: Når er man for syk til å jobbe?
headline_en: When are you too sick to work?
lead_en: The article explains when workers should stay home sick and how self-certification works in Norway. An expert says the key question is whether you can still do your job safely and sensibly, taking symptoms and risk of infecting others into account.
justification: The article is primarily about sickness absence, workplace health, and practical guidance on when illness justifies staying home from work.

headline_no: T-bane-avsporinger: Mye trafikk og tørre skinner trolig årsaken
headline_en: Metro derailments: Heavy traffic and dry rails likely the cause
lead_en: Sporveien says recent Oslo metro derailments were likely caused by unusually heavy traffic in some parts of the network and overly dry rails in others. The company says there is no single clear cause and that the metro remains safe to use.
justification: The article is mainly about public transport operations and technical explanations for derailments, which do not fit the main election issue categories, with a minor governance angle because it concerns infrastructure management by a public operator.

headline_no: Én uke før valget handler valgkampen om kaos på borgerlig side
headline_en: One week before the election, the campaign is about chaos on the centre-right
lead_en: The commentary argues that the centre-right election campaign has become dominated by internal chaos and poor coordination just one week before the vote. This infighting may demobilize their voters and hand Labour an advantage in the final stretch.
justification: The article is primarily about election dynamics, leadership, coalition credibility, and the prime minister question on the centre-right, which fits governance and campaign competence.

headline_no: Redningsaksjon etter skred avsluttet
headline_en: Rescue operation after landslide called off
lead_en: A man is presumed dead after a landslide struck the E6 in Levanger on Saturday morning. The area remains unstable, major roads and rail are closed, and concerns are being raised about prior knowledge of the ground conditions.
justification: The article is primarily a factual report on a fatal landslide and infrastructure disruption, not on one of the main campaign policy categories listed.

headline_no: Banebrytende norsk forskning om hjerteinfarkt
headline_en: Groundbreaking Norwegian research on heart attacks
lead_en: Norwegian researchers say a major new study could change heart attack treatment worldwide by showing that beta blockers still benefit many patients after infarction. The findings are based on data from Norwegian and Danish survivors and may shape new international guidelines.
justification: The article is primarily about medical research, heart attack treatment, and healthcare practice, which fits the health and public services category.

headline_no: Ble kontaktet av Tiktok om falske Frp-kontoer
headline_en: Was contacted by TikTok about fake FrP accounts
lead_en: TikTok contacted the Progress Party about fake accounts using the party’s name and branding, but the party did not respond until Aftenposten raised the issue. Many of the accounts are still active just days before the parliamentary election.
justification: The article is primarily about party management, campaign integrity, and democratic accountability around fake political accounts, which fits governance and political competence.

headline_no: Venstre-lederen om Erna Solbergs statsministeravklaring: – Det eneste realistiske
headline_en: The Liberal Party leader on Erna Solberg’s clarification on the prime minister question: “The only realistic option”
lead_en: Venstre leader Guri Melby backs Erna Solberg’s claim that she is the only realistic non-socialist prime minister candidate if the centre-right wins a majority. The dispute highlights election strategy, voter mobilization, and tensions between Venstre, Høyre, and Frp.
justification: The article is mainly about prime ministerial leadership, coalition dynamics, and electoral strategy on the centre-right, which fits governance rather than a substantive policy area.

headline_no: Smitteverntopper trekker seg i protest mot Robert F. Kennedy jr.
headline_en: Top infectious disease officials resign in protest against Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
lead_en: Senior officials at the U.S. CDC have resigned after clashes with Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. over vaccine policy and what they describe as unscientific directives. The dispute has raised warnings about public health and national health security in the United States.
justification: The article is primarily about vaccine policy, public health administration, and healthcare leadership, which fits the health category rather than Norwegian election politics.

headline_no: Lang Plussnatts ferd mot dag
headline_en: The long PlussNatt journey toward day
lead_en: The writer tests Vy’s overnight PlussNatt service on the Bergen Line and finds the seat comfortable, but the experience is undermined by noisy fellow passengers and too little privacy. In the end, he concludes the product is good, though better suited to travelers who come prepared.
justification: The article is a consumer-style feature about train travel and passenger experience, which does not fit the main political issue categories.

headline_no: Norske produsenter kaster seg på alkoholfri drikketrend: – Folk har begynt å kreve mer.
headline_en: Norwegian producers jump on the non-alcoholic drinks trend: “People have started demanding more.”
lead_en: Norwegian producers of non-alcoholic drinks are expanding rapidly as consumer demand grows, with some now supplying Vinmonopolet and Michelin restaurants. The trend is driven by interest in health, moderation, and more sophisticated alcohol-free options.
justification: The article is mainly a business and lifestyle feature about the growth of the non-alcoholic drinks market, with a secondary health angle related to moderation and wellness.

headline_no: Oljefond, valgkamp og politisk risiko
headline_en: The sovereign wealth fund, the election campaign, and political risk
lead_en: The author argues that the sovereign wealth fund has clumsily exposed itself and its political superiors to controversy by allowing its Israel-related investments to become a sensitive issue during the election campaign. This has pulled the fund into sharp national and international political debate.
justification: The article is primarily about the sovereign wealth fund’s Israel-related investments and the political backlash surrounding Gaza/Israel, while also touching on election-campaign governance and political management.

headline_no: Politiet mener Bhatti var mesterhjernen bak Oslo-skytingen
headline_en: Police believe Bhatti was the mastermind behind the Oslo shooting
lead_en: Prosecutors say Arfan Bhatti helped procure weapons for Zaniar Matapour and was the main figure behind the 2022 Oslo terror attack. His trial begins in Oslo District Court, and he faces up to 30 years in preventive detention.
justification: The article centers on terrorism, violent extremism, and criminal prosecution in Norway, which fits the crime-related dimension of IMM, with a secondary security dimension because it concerns a terror case.

headline_no: Forsvar: Norge kjøper fregatter fra Storbritannia for 136 milliarder kroner. – Dette hander om trygghet for landet, sier Jonas Gahr Støre (Ap).
headline_en: Defence: Norway is buying frigates from the United Kingdom for NOK 136 billion. “This is about security for the country,” says Jonas Gahr Støre (Labour).
lead_en: Norway has chosen British Type 26 frigates in a deal worth about NOK 136 billion, which Støre says is fundamentally about national security. The purchase is framed as a strategic defence partnership with the UK, though critics raise concerns about cost, delays, and Israeli components.
justification: The article is primarily about defence procurement, NATO-aligned security cooperation, and Norway’s strategic military partnership with the UK, with a secondary leadership/governance element because the decision is tied to government choice and political positioning.

headline_no: Britiske båter høres bra ut
headline_en: British ships sound like a good choice
lead_en: The commentator argues that Norway’s decision to buy British frigates appears sensible because identical vessels with close allies should make operations, maintenance, and strategic cooperation easier. The piece also notes geopolitical risks, including possible U.S. irritation.
justification: The article is mainly an evaluation of Norway’s frigate purchase and defence cooperation with close NATO allies, making security and defence the dominant issue.

headline_no: Er stor-investor i selskaper som hugger regnskog
headline_en: Is a major investor in companies that are cutting down rainforest
lead_en: A Brazilian mining company plans to clear large areas of Amazon rainforest to expand a bauxite mine, while Norway’s sovereign wealth fund remains heavily invested in two of its owners. The article highlights criticism that the fund ignored an ethics recommendation to divest despite serious environmental risks.
justification: The article is primarily about rainforest destruction, environmental harm, and the tension between Norway’s climate commitments and the sovereign wealth fund’s investments, with a secondary governance aspect because it critiques official oversight and decision-making.

headline_no: Trump kan straffe Norge for Oljefondets fy-liste
headline_en: Trump could punish Norway over the sovereign wealth fund’s blacklist
lead_en: The article warns that Norway could face major costs if Donald Trump reacts to the sovereign wealth fund’s exclusion of U.S. companies, especially Caterpillar over its links to Israeli actions in Gaza and the West Bank. Politicians and experts fear possible retaliation through tariffs, taxes, or other financial measures.
justification: The article is primarily about fallout from the sovereign wealth fund’s exclusions tied to Israel/Gaza, with a secondary foreign-policy and security dimension because it focuses on possible U.S. retaliation against Norway.

headline_no: Venezuelas husmødre og pensjonister gjør seg klar til krig mot USA
headline_en: Venezuela’s housewives and pensioners are preparing for war against the United States
lead_en: As tensions rise with the United States, Venezuelans are volunteering for militia service after Nicolás Maduro called on the public to defend the country. The article examines fears of a possible U.S. attack and how the external threat could strengthen Maduro politically.
justification: The article is primarily about international conflict, military escalation, and U.S.–Venezuela relations, which squarely fits security and foreign policy.

headline_no: Asylhoteller skaper sinne og protester
headline_en: Asylum hotels are creating anger and protests
lead_en: Protests over asylum hotels in Britain intensified after a case involving an asylum seeker fueled an already heated immigration debate. The article explains how migrant crossings, hotel accommodation, and Nigel Farage’s campaigning have made immigration the country’s most salient issue.
justification: The article is primarily about asylum seekers, migration, and public backlash against immigration policy in the UK, with a secondary governance element because it also covers party competition and state responses.

headline_no: De får nyhetene sine fra Tiktok, men sluker ikke det de blir servert
headline_en: They get their news from TikTok, but they do not swallow everything they are served
lead_en: A commentator meets first-time voters who use TikTok and other social media for political information but also fact-check, read news articles, and compare voting guides before deciding. The piece portrays them as more critical and thoughtful than stereotypes suggest.
justification: The article is mainly about voter behavior, party choice, campaign information, and perceptions of party leaders rather than a specific policy issue, so it fits governance and electoral politics.

headline_no: Mangler tog. Kaos for pendlere.
headline_en: Not enough trains. Chaos for commuters.
lead_en: Commuters on busy rail lines around Oslo are regularly facing severely overcrowded trains because departures planned with double trainsets are often run at half capacity. The article links the problems to maintenance backlogs, delayed new trains, and limited workshop capacity.
justification: The article is primarily about failures in a core public service—commuter rail transport—and their consequences for passengers, with a secondary governance element because responsibility is split across public bodies and operators.

headline_no: Rasende på fulle trikker? Det er til ditt eget beste!
headline_en: Angry about packed trams? It is for your own good!
lead_en: In a satirical commentary, the writer mocks Oslo’s chaotic and overcrowded public transport system, especially after the closure of Majorstuen station. The piece jokes that commuters are being “stress-tested” by confusing replacement services and dangerously full trams.
justification: The article is primarily about overcrowded and poorly functioning public transport as a public service problem, with a secondary governance element because it satirizes municipal planning and service management.

headline_no: Mannen som ble tatt av skredet, jobbet på oppdrag for Bane Nor
headline_en: The man taken by the landslide was working on assignment for Bane Nor
lead_en: A Danish contractor working for Bane Nor is presumed dead after a quick-clay landslide in Levanger swept away roads and the Nordland railway line. Authorities and investigators are now examining the cause while major transport disruptions are expected to last for months.
justification: The article is mainly about a fatal infrastructure accident and disruption to core public transport services, with a secondary governance element because public agencies and Bane Nor are responsible for the response and investigation.

headline_no: Kraftig fall i brunostsalget
headline_en: Sharp decline in brown cheese sales
lead_en: Norwegians have cut their brown cheese consumption significantly over the past 15 years, although exports of the traditional product have risen strongly. Industry and nutrition experts point to changing eating habits and new alternatives in the spread market.
justification: The article is mainly about changing food consumption and exports of a traditional Norwegian product, which does not fit the main political issue categories.

headline_no: De har vaert bestevenner i et halvt århundre. Én dag sto det om livet.
headline_en: They have been best friends for half a century. One day, it was a matter of life and death.
lead_en: The article follows two elderly women in Oslo whose 50-year friendship became life-saving when one suffered a stroke and the other had the key to let help in. It reflects on how deep friendship can be as important as romantic love.
justification: The article is primarily a human-interest feature about friendship and aging rather than a political issue, with a secondary health angle because a stroke emergency is central to the story.

headline_no: Ble hedret av tusenvis av mennesker i et hav av gule blomster
headline_en: Honored by thousands of people in a sea of yellow flowers
lead_en: Several thousand people gathered in Oslo for a memorial march for Tamima Nibras Juhar after a killing described as politically motivated and linked to far-right views. Speakers, including Prime Minister Jonas Gahr Støre, framed the event as a collective rejection of hatred and racism.
justification: The article centers on a racist, far-right motivated killing and the public response to hate and extremism, which fits best under the crime/integration dimension of IMM.

headline_no: Plukk ikke fra hverandre fregattene
headline_en: Do not take the frigates apart
lead_en: The government’s plan to buy British frigates for NOK 135 billion is presented as the right choice for Norway’s navy. The editorial argues that close defence cooperation with the UK and identical vessels will strengthen maintenance and operational collaboration.
justification: The article primarily concerns defence procurement and Norway’s naval security strategy, while secondarily touching on Gaza through debate over Israeli components in the ships.

headline_no: 1 milliard for å begynne i ny jobb? Bli Ki-geni!
headline_en: 1 billion to start a new job? Become an AI genius!
lead_en: The U.S. is pouring enormous sums into artificial intelligence, with top experts being offered billion-kroner pay packages. The article asks whether this investment frenzy is justified or whether it resembles a dangerous bubble.
justification: The article is primarily about the AI boom in the United States and its broader technological implications rather than a core Norwegian election issue, with a secondary economic angle due to discussion of investment bubbles and market effects.

headline_no: Urokråken som tror han kan bremse Trump
headline_en: The maverick who thinks he can slow Trump down
lead_en: Bernie Sanders, 83, is touring the United States to push back against Donald Trump and recruit candidates for future elections. The article focuses on Democratic Party strategy, voter enthusiasm, and possible contenders for 2026 and 2028.
justification: The article is primarily about party strategy, electoral mobilisation, leadership, and future candidacies in U.S. politics, with only a secondary reference to healthcare policy.

headline_no: Skal sikre nettet mot neste krise
headline_en: Will secure the network against the next crisis
lead_en: Norway’s digital infrastructure is vulnerable to extreme weather, sabotage, and outages, as shown when storm Hans cut internet and phone access in Hallingdal in 2023. The government is now promising faster investment in digital preparedness and backup systems.
justification: The article is primarily about national digital resilience and preparedness against crises, sabotage, and signal interference, making security the dominant issue, with a secondary governance angle through the government’s new plan and funding.

headline_no: Bevisene mot Høiby: Dette skal statsadvokaten legge frem i retten
headline_en: The evidence against Høiby: What the public prosecutor will present in court
lead_en: Prosecutors plan to use smartwatch data, health-app information, and a sleep expert to try to convict Marius Borg Høiby on several charges, including four alleged rapes. The case will hinge in part on whether the complainants were asleep during the assaults.
justification: The article is primarily a criminal court report about evidence in the Høiby case, which does not fit the main election-related policy categories, though it has a minor governance/legal-system dimension.

headline_no: Gir Norge nok hjelp til Ukraina? Nå er mye av pengene brukt.
headline_en: Is Norway giving enough help to Ukraine? Now much of the money has been spent.
lead_en: During a visit to Kyiv, Jonas Gahr Støre faced pressure from Volodymyr Zelenskyj to use Norway’s remaining Ukraine funds this year on drones. The article examines how Norway’s support has been allocated, whether it is sufficient, and concerns about corruption in Ukraine.
justification: The article is primarily about Norway’s military and financial support for Ukraine in the context of war, defence, and international relations, with a secondary governance angle related to allocation decisions and corruption oversight.

headline_no: Tenker nytt om eldreomsorg – nå vil danskene kopiere modellen
headline_en: Rethinking elderly care – now the Danes want to copy the model
lead_en: Lillehammer has developed a home-based elderly care model with mobile emergency care and close follow-up, aiming to reduce hospital admissions and make care more efficient. The government is highlighting it as a possible model for other municipalities.
justification: The article is primarily about healthcare and elderly care reform, focusing on new service delivery models to handle aging and reduce hospital pressure, with a secondary governance angle through national scaling and funding structures.

headline_no: Politiet starter etterforskning etter Levanger-ras
headline_en: Police open investigation after the Levanger landslide
lead_en: Police are investigating the Levanger landslide as a possible fatal workplace accident after a Danish man is believed to have died. Investigators will examine whether work in the area and underlying ground conditions helped trigger the slide.
justification: The article is primarily a news report about a fatal landslide investigation and possible workplace accident, with only a secondary environmental angle related to unstable ground and quick clay.

headline_no: Hva i alle dager var dette?
headline_en: What on earth was this?
lead_en: The article criticizes Prime Minister Jonas Gahr Støre for commenting prematurely on an ongoing murder investigation and other police cases. It argues that a prime minister should not speculate about guilt or motives before the legal process is complete.
justification: The article is primarily about leadership, role understanding, and the prime minister’s handling of ongoing criminal cases, making governance and political competence the central issue.

headline_no: Reagerer på Støres kommentar om drapet på Kampen
headline_en: Reacts to Støre’s comment about the killing at Kampen
lead_en: Jonas Gahr Støre was criticized after linking the killing at Kampen to a “privatized arrangement” with poor staffing and security during a party leaders’ debate. Venstre argues that the prime minister was politicizing a very serious ongoing case.
justification: The article is primarily about Støre’s judgment, leadership, and political handling of a sensitive ongoing case during the campaign, making governance and competence the central issue.

headline_no: Politiinspektør Knut Rød er verkebyllen som aldri blir borte i rettsoppgjøret etter krigen.
headline_en: Police inspector Knut Rød is the sore that never disappears from the postwar legal reckoning
lead_en: The article discusses two new books about police inspector Knut Rød, who helped organize the arrest and deportation of Norwegian Jews in 1942 but was acquitted after the war. It argues that his case remains a lasting national trauma and moral stain in Norway.
justification: The article is primarily a historical commentary on Norway’s postwar justice process and the deportation of Jews, which does not fit the main election issue categories, with only a secondary contemporary reference to Gaza and antisemitism.

headline_no: Over 800 døde i jordskjelv i Afghanistan
headline_en: More than 800 dead in earthquake in Afghanistan
lead_en: A powerful earthquake struck the Afghanistan-Pakistan border region late Sunday, killing at least 800 people and injuring more than 2,500, according to Afghan authorities. Rescue work is being hampered by difficult terrain, landslides, and flooding.
justification: The article is a foreign disaster report about an earthquake in Afghanistan and does not fit the main Norwegian election issue categories.

headline_no: Har du 120.000 kroner til overs? Da kan du bo en natt her.
headline_en: Do you have 120,000 kroner to spare? Then you can stay here for one night.
lead_en: Oslo and Norway are attracting more affluent tourists, and Grand Hotel has launched what is likely the country’s most expensive suite at NOK 120,000 per night. The article describes growing demand for ultra-luxury travel and high-end hotel offerings in the capital.
justification: The article is primarily about tourism demand, luxury spending, and the market for high-end hospitality in Norway, making the economy the closest fit, with some broader lifestyle and travel content.

headline_no: Nybilsalget i august er dårlig nytt for Teslas konkurrenter
headline_en: New car sales in August are bad news for Tesla’s competitors
lead_en: New car registrations in Norway rose sharply in August, with electric vehicles making up 94.5 percent of sales so far this year. While Tesla sales have weakened elsewhere in Europe, the company remains strong in the Norwegian market.
