justification: The article is primarily about electric vehicle adoption and the transition away from fossil-fuel cars in Norway, with a secondary economic dimension related to sales trends, market shares, and financing.

headline_no: Slik har amerikanske studenter gjort søsterskap til pengeindustri
headline_en: How American students turned sororities into a money-making industry
lead_en: The article examines how sorority recruitment in the United States has become a lucrative social-media-driven business, with students, influencers, and consultants profiting from “rush season.” It also highlights criticism of the culture for promoting exclusion, racism, and hyper-consumerism.
justification: The article is a cultural and social feature about U.S. student life, influencer economics, and sorority culture, which does not fit the main Norwegian election issue categories.

headline_no: Derfor kan en kiwi vaere det perfekte mellommåltidet
headline_en: Why a kiwi can be the perfect snack
lead_en: Kiwis are rich in fiber, vitamin C, and other nutrients, and nutrition experts say they can support digestion and overall health. The article also examines claims that kiwi may help with sleep, while noting the evidence is still limited.
justification: The article is primarily about nutrition and health effects, focusing on digestion, sleep, and the health benefits of kiwi fruit.

headline_no: Han bryter ned demokratiet bit for bit
headline_en: He is dismantling democracy bit by bit
lead_en: Donald Trump denies being authoritarian, but Aftenposten argues that his actions show growing political control over institutions meant to remain independent. The editorial warns that this erosion of U.S. democracy is serious, also for Norway because of the countries’ close economic and security ties.
justification: The article is primarily about developments in the United States with implications for Norway’s foreign-policy and security relationship with its most important ally, while secondarily addressing democratic governance and institutional independence.

headline_no: Rakel Haugen Strand: Handler formuesskatten om liv eller død for norsk økonomi? Noe skurrer.
headline_en: Rakel Haugen Strand: Is the wealth tax a matter of life or death for the Norwegian economy? Something does not add up.
lead_en: The debate over the wealth tax has become overheated and at times absurd, drowning out the substantive arguments. The columnist asks how harmful the tax really is and argues for a more fact-based, broad tax settlement after the election.
justification: The article’s main focus is the Norwegian wealth tax debate, its effects on entrepreneurs, inequality, and redistribution, with a secondary connection to broader economic performance.

headline_no: Risikerer lovens strengeste straff
headline_en: Faces the harshest penalty under the law
lead_en: Three years after being charged with aiding terrorism, Arfan Bhatti appeared in Oslo District Court accused of helping plan the 2022 London Pub attack. Prosecutors say he was a key figure behind the attack, while Bhatti denies all criminal guilt.
justification: The article is primarily about a major terrorism trial and questions of national security and violent extremism in Norway.

headline_no: Influensere er Israels nye våpen
headline_en: Influencers are Israel’s new weapon
lead_en: Aftenposten reports that Israel has brought American influencers to Gaza as part of a broader effort to rebuild support among young Americans. The article argues that these visits spread misleading claims and ignore the deadly realities surrounding aid distribution in Gaza.
justification: The article is primarily about the Gaza war, Israel’s messaging strategy, and plans concerning Gaza’s future, which fits the Gaza/Palestine category, with a secondary foreign-policy dimension.

headline_no: Mens guttene flokker seg om høyresiden, strømmer unge jenter til MDG
headline_en: While boys flock to the right, young women are streaming to the Green Party
lead_en: A new poll shows MDG at a record high, driven especially by strong support among young women, while young men are moving heavily toward the right, especially the Progress Party. MDG’s deputy leader says an important end-of-campaign message is to help ensure Jonas Gahr Støre remains prime minister.
justification: The article is mainly about polling, party competition, and government formation on the eve of the election, with climate policy as a secondary theme through MDG’s positioning.

headline_no: Listhaugs bobil kjører forbi Vedums traktor
headline_en: Listhaug’s motorhome drives past Vedum’s tractor
lead_en: New polling data show the Progress Party performing strongest in Norway’s smallest municipalities, while the Centre Party is losing ground in its old rural heartland. The article argues that Sylvi Listhaug has become the new voice of dissatisfied rural voters, though the election remains close overall.
justification: The article is mainly about polling, voter shifts, party competition, and who may govern after the election, with a secondary link to cost-of-living grievances driving protest voting.

headline_no: Ber ikke kronprinsesse Mette-marit om å vitne
headline_en: Will not ask Crown Princess Mette-Marit to testify
lead_en: Defence lawyers in the trial against Marius Borg Høiby say they will not call his mother, Crown Princess Mette-Marit, as a witness. The article focuses on the forthcoming criminal trial, the charges against Høiby, and the handling of possible witnesses.
justification: The article is primarily a court and royal-family news story about a criminal case, which does not fit the main election issue categories and is therefore best coded as Other.

headline_no: Taliban ber om hjelp – mer enn 1400 døde
headline_en: Taliban asks for help – more than 1,400 dead
lead_en: At least 1,411 people have been killed and 3,124 injured after an earthquake struck northeastern Afghanistan. Taliban authorities are appealing for international help as rescue efforts continue in remote, badly damaged areas.
justification: The article is primarily about a natural disaster and humanitarian emergency in Afghanistan, which does not fit the main election-related issue categories.

headline_no: Nå skal finnene omsider fase ut hakekorset
headline_en: Now the Finns will finally phase out the swastika
lead_en: Finland is ending the use of swastika symbols in parts of its air force after years of controversy and awkward situations with foreign visitors. The change comes as Finland is more closely integrated with NATO allies, including Norway.
justification: The article is primarily about Finland’s air force, NATO integration, and military cooperation with allies including Norway, making security/defence the main issue.

headline_no: De er enige om en ny verdensorden
headline_en: They agree on a new world order
lead_en: A summit in China showed Xi Jinping, Vladimir Putin, and Narendra Modi drawing closer around the idea of a more multipolar world and reduced Western dominance. The article presents the meeting as a strategic success for all three leaders, especially China.
justification: The article is primarily about global power shifts, foreign policy, and strategic alignment among major powers, with a secondary economic dimension through trade conflict, energy, and development financing.

headline_no: 70 legar slutta på Haukeland
headline_en: 70 doctors quit Haukeland
lead_en: Around 70 doctors have left Haukeland University Hospital over the past two years, alarming union representatives and hospital management. The article highlights staffing shortages, heavy workloads, and fears that worsening retention will harm patient care.
justification: The article is primarily about hospital staffing, healthcare capacity, and the consequences of losing medical personnel in Norway’s public health system.

headline_no: Oslos ordfører: – Ufattelig drøyt at jeg er kopiert inn i invitasjonen
headline_en: Oslo’s mayor: “It is unbelievably outrageous that I am copied into the invitation”
lead_en: Oslo’s mayor Anne Lindboe condemns a new planned “walk” on Grønland organized by the Norway Democrats with a politician from Germany’s AfD, calling it a deliberate provocation. The event is framed around immigration and alleged “no-go” areas, and comes shortly after a killing linked to far-right extremism.
justification: The article is primarily about anti-immigration mobilization, “remigration,” and claims about immigrant-dominated urban areas, with a secondary governance dimension because it also features the mayor’s response and public-order concerns.

headline_no: Milliardaersøstrene: Dette er Sophie og Jenny Spetalen
headline_en: The billionaire sisters: This is Sophie and Jenny Spetalen
lead_en: Billionaire investor Øystein Stray Spetalen has transferred major shareholdings to his daughters, instantly making Sophie and Jenny Spetalen two of Norway’s richest women. The article profiles the sisters and places the transfer in the context of wealth, inheritance, and recent tax-related debates.
justification: The article mainly concerns large private wealth transfers within one of Norway’s richest families and explicitly connects them to inheritance, wealth, and the broader tax context.

headline_no: – Lover og normer blir mer diffuse når man er utenfor Norge
headline_en: “Laws and norms become more blurred when you are outside Norway”
lead_en: After a former diplomat child described abuse and neglect during an overseas upbringing, a researcher says such problems are not surprising in international expat environments. The article examines the Norwegian foreign service’s responsibility toward employees’ children posted abroad.
justification: The article mainly concerns institutional responsibility and accountability within Norway’s foreign service, making governance the best fit, with a secondary social/welfare dimension.

headline_no: Hvilken fred, Xi Jinping?
headline_en: What peace, Xi Jinping?
lead_en: Xi Jinping says the world must choose between peace and war, but the article argues this rings hollow as he celebrates with Vladimir Putin and Kim Jong-un while China continues backing Russia politically and economically.
justification: The article is primarily about international security, authoritarian alignment, and China’s support for Russia’s war against Ukraine, with a secondary leadership and regime-competence angle.

headline_no: Rå og uforfalsket propaganda er tilbake for fullt
headline_en: Crude and unfiltered propaganda is back in full force
lead_en: The article warns that anonymous social media campaigns are using hate, deception, and algorithm-driven outrage to influence Norway’s 2025 election. It argues this marks a disturbing new phase in Norwegian political communication.
justification: The article is primarily about the integrity of the election campaign, anonymous influence operations, and how these affect democratic debate and political leadership in Norway.

headline_no: 800 kjempet om jobb hos McDonald's
headline_en: 800 competed for a job at McDonald’s
lead_en: A young woman in Oslo spent nine months looking for part-time work before landing a job at McDonald’s, where 800 applicants competed for 74 positions. The article uses her case to highlight young people’s pessimism about the labor market and rising barriers to entry.
justification: The article is primarily about economic insecurity and access to work for young people, focusing on a difficult labor market and worries about future job prospects.

headline_no: Hvilken fred, Xi Jinping?
headline_en: What peace, Xi Jinping?
lead_en: Xi Jinping says the world faces a choice between peace and war, but the article argues this is hollow rhetoric when China honors Putin and Kim while supporting Russia’s war politically and economically.
justification: The article is mainly about foreign policy, authoritarian power alignment, and China’s stance toward Russia’s war in Ukraine, with a secondary focus on leadership and political positioning.

headline_no: Støre fester grepet i ny måling før valget
headline_en: Støre tightens his grip in new poll before the election
lead_en: A new poll for Aftenposten and VG shows Jonas Gahr Støre strengthening his lead as preferred prime minister over both Erna Solberg and Sylvi Listhaug just days before the election. The article frames him as better able to unite and lead than his right-wing rivals.
justification: The article is primarily about leadership, prime minister preferences, and election dynamics rather than any specific policy issue.

headline_no: Listhaug er blitt et enda større problem
headline_en: Listhaug has become an even bigger problem
lead_en: A new poll suggests Sylvi Listhaug has become less attractive as a prime minister candidate, losing support even among voters in parties Frp would need to cooperate with. The commentary argues she is now a greater burden for the non-socialist bloc.
justification: The article is primarily about leadership, coalition viability, and prime minister preferences in the election campaign, not a substantive policy issue.

headline_no: Hunden Jessie ble et symbol som gir ukrainere håp
headline_en: The dog Jessie became a symbol that gives Ukrainians hope
lead_en: After a Russian strike killed her owner in Kyiv, the dog Jessie kept howling outside the ruined apartment block for two days and became a symbol of hope for many Ukrainians. The article places her story within intensifying Russian attacks enabled by support from North Korea and China.
justification: The article is primarily about Russia’s war against Ukraine, foreign support for Russian attacks, and the broader security consequences of the escalating air war.

headline_no: PST kartlegger: – Ofte svaert få biter i puslespillet
headline_en: PST maps threats: “Often very few pieces in the puzzle”
lead_en: The article explains how Norway’s security police assess radicalized individuals and place them in a threat pyramid, often based on very limited information. It highlights the challenge of distinguishing extreme views from a real willingness to use violence.
justification: The article is primarily about terrorism prevention, radicalization, and domestic security monitoring by PST, with a secondary governance angle related to state threat assessment.

headline_no: Bhatti i tårer da han snakket om sin avdøde sønn
headline_en: Bhatti in tears when he spoke about his deceased son
lead_en: In court, Arfan Bhatti gave emotional testimony about his life and broke down when asked about his son, who was killed in Syria. The article is centered on his defense testimony in the terror trial linked to the 25 June 2022 attack in Oslo.
justification: The article primarily concerns a terrorism case, radicalization, and courtroom proceedings related to the Oslo attack, with a secondary governance angle because it deals with the justice system and prosecution.

headline_no: IS sprengte det hellige bygget i luften. Nå er det gjenreist.
headline_en: ISIS blew the holy building to pieces. Now it has been rebuilt.
lead_en: Al-Nuri Mosque in Mosul, where ISIS proclaimed its caliphate in 2014, has reopened after being destroyed by the group during the battle for the city. The article uses the rebuilding to reflect on ISIS’s rise, fall, and the wider legacy of war in Iraq and Syria.
justification: The article is primarily about terrorism, conflict, and the rise and defeat of ISIS, with a secondary focus on cultural reconstruction after war.

headline_no: ALS-pasient klaget på vedtak – bydelen svarte med redusert tilbud
headline_en: ALS patient appealed a decision – the borough responded with a reduced offer
lead_en: An ALS patient in Oslo won support from the County Governor, who said her care offer was below a defensible minimum level, but the borough then proposed fewer home-care hours and digital monitoring instead. The article highlights her struggle for round-the-clock assistance at home.
justification: The article is primarily about healthcare and welfare services, focusing on access to adequate home care for a seriously ill patient, with a secondary governance angle about unequal municipal provision and administrative decisions.

headline_no: Slik endrer Donald Trump utseendet til USAs hovedstad
headline_en: This is how Donald Trump is changing the appearance of the U.S. capital
lead_en: The article describes how Donald Trump is reshaping Washington’s look, from giant banners and beautification projects to major aesthetic changes at the White House. It presents these visual interventions as part of his broader political imprint on the capital.
justification: The article is mainly about Trump’s leadership style and symbolic use of state power in reshaping the capital, rather than a substantive policy area.

headline_no: Ekspertenes råd om hvem som bør velge norgespris
headline_en: Experts’ advice on who should choose the Norway price
lead_en: Experts say households in Southern Norway should opt for the fixed-price electricity scheme, while people in Mid- and Northern Norway should probably avoid it because market prices there are expected to stay lower. The article focuses on how geography shapes whether the new power-price policy will pay off.
justification: The article is primarily about electricity prices, household costs, and the economic effects of the government’s new fixed-price power scheme.

headline_no: Klimaendringene kan gå hardt ut over boligforsikringen
headline_en: Climate change could hit home insurance hard
lead_en: The article explains that more floods and landslides in Norway may drive up home-insurance costs as climate-related damage becomes more frequent. It also raises concerns about whether today’s evenly shared insurance model can hold if high-risk building continues.
justification: The article is primarily about the domestic consequences of climate change, especially increased flood and landslide risk, with a secondary economic angle through rising insurance costs.

headline_no: Venter få rentekutt og kraftig vekst i boligpriser
headline_en: Expect few rate cuts and strong growth in house prices
lead_en: Nordea Markets says the Norwegian economy is strengthening and expects only limited interest-rate cuts after this year. Even so, it warns that lower rates and rising incomes could push house prices up sharply through 2027.
justification: The article is primarily about interest rates, economic growth, purchasing power, and the housing market in Norway.

headline_no: Får full lønn i syv år – som førtidspensjonist
headline_en: Gets full salary for seven years – as an early retiree
lead_en: The article reveals that Nordkraft CEO Eirik Frantzen is entitled to full pay from age 60 to 67, potentially worth around 30 million kroner, despite weak company results and no dividend payouts to municipal owners. It raises sharp questions about oversight, bonus arrangements, and board governance.
justification: The article is primarily about governance and oversight failures around executive pay in a municipally owned power company, with a secondary inequality and compensation angle.

headline_no: Verdens største skipstunnel bør forbli en drøm
headline_en: The world's largest ship tunnel should remain a dream
lead_en: Residents and politicians face renewed uncertainty over the Stad ship tunnel after new bids showed the project will cost far more than approved. The editorial argues that the tunnel is economically unsound and should be abandoned.
justification: The article primarily centers on public spending, cost overruns, and whether the state should prioritize funding this infrastructure project, making economic governance the dominant issue.

headline_no: Kaos er det mest stabile
headline_en: Chaos is the most stable
lead_en: The commentator argues that Norway’s many small parties are a democratic strength, because they force negotiation and make more voters feel represented. In contrast, he warns that majoritarian systems in countries like the UK, France, and the US can fuel instability and populism.
justification: The article is primarily about democratic representation, party system design, and coalition politics rather than a specific policy area, so governance is the dominant issue.

headline_no: Landesorg i Portugal etter kabeltrikkulykken
headline_en: National mourning in Portugal after the cable tram दुर्घटना
lead_en: Portuguese authorities are investigating why a cable tram in central Lisbon derailed and crashed down a steep hill, killing at least 16 people. The government has declared national mourning as rescue workers and investigators respond to the disaster.
justification: The article is primarily a foreign news report about a transport accident in Portugal and does not center on any of the listed Norwegian election issue categories.

headline_no: Stjernekamp om stortingsplasser
headline_en: Star battle over parliamentary seats
lead_en: New polling suggests that several high-profile politicians, especially in Høyre and the Centre Party, risk losing their seats in parliament. The article highlights how this could affect party renewal, leadership succession, and post-election strategy.
justification: The article is mainly about polling, parliamentary seat allocation, party leadership, and post-election positioning, which makes governance and political competition the primary issue.

headline_no: Gratisferger kommer ikke uten følger. Hard kamp om plassene.
headline_en: Free ferries do not come without consequences. A hard fight for places.
lead_en: Residents save large sums from free ferry travel, but the policy has also led to sharply higher traffic and long queues, sometimes leaving locals stranded. The article examines both the personal financial benefits and the wider costs for transport capacity and public budgets.
justification: The article is primarily about the economic effects of a government subsidy scheme—household savings, increased demand, and strained transport funding—with a secondary focus on policy implementation and governance.

headline_no: Nordmann pågrepet etter dobbeltdrap i Sverige
headline_en: Norwegian man arrested after double murder in Sweden
lead_en: A Norwegian man was arrested in Oslo in connection with a double killing in Gothenburg after police followed him from the Swedish border. Norwegian police say the arrest was dramatic and that he threatened officers with a firearm.
justification: The article is primarily about a violent cross-border criminal case and police action, which fits the crime dimension of the immigration/integration/crime category.

headline_no: 270 småkommuner kan miste millionbeløp
headline_en: 270 small municipalities could lose millions
lead_en: Høyre wants to abolish the subsidy for small local schools and redistribute the money by population, which would shift large sums from smaller municipalities to bigger cities. The debate centers on whether school funding should protect rural schools or be redirected to municipalities with broader welfare pressures.
justification: The article is primarily about school funding and the provision of local public services, with a secondary governance dimension concerning how state transfers are distributed.

headline_no: Oslo: Store partiprofiler i valgthriller
headline_en: Oslo: Major party profiles in an election thriller
lead_en: A new Oslo poll shows a tight battle for the final parliamentary seats, with Bjørnar Moxnes barely holding on while prominent candidates from SV, Høyre, MDG, and KrF challenge him. The poll also shows Labour has regained its position as Oslo’s largest party.
justification: The article is primarily about polling, seat allocation, party competition, and electoral strategy in Oslo, which makes governance and horse-race politics the dominant issue.

headline_no: ChatGPT + MDG = sant
headline_en: ChatGPT + MDG = a perfect match
lead_en: Aftenposten’s test finds that ChatGPT’s answers in Norwegian voting guides align most closely with MDG and generally lean toward the left. The article raises concerns about how AI tools may shape voters’ choices and democratic debate.
justification: The article is primarily about democratic influence, election guidance, and the role of AI platforms in shaping political choices, which makes governance the dominant issue.

headline_no: Bhatti avviser terrorsnakk med Matapour
headline_en: Bhatti rejects claims of terror talk with Matapour
lead_en: In court, Arfan Bhatti denied discussing terrorism with Zaniar Matapour and said he only learned of the 25 June 2022 Oslo attack the next morning. He admitted trying to pass on a voice message to whom he believed was ISIS, but said this was a mistake and denied guilt.
justification: The article is primarily about a terrorism-related criminal case and court testimony, which fits most closely under the crime dimension of the immigration/integration/crime category.

headline_no: Voldsvideoer flyter fritt. Det får tragiske konsekvenser.
headline_en: Violent videos flow freely. That has tragic consequences.
lead_en: The commentary argues that young people are being drawn into online cultures obsessed with violence, and that some later emerge as terrorists. It links recent attacks in the US and Oslo to broader patterns of online radicalization and extremist subcultures.
justification: The article primarily focuses on radicalization, terrorism, and the security threat posed by violent online extremist milieus, with crime as a secondary dimension.

headline_no: – Jeg var 14 år første gang jeg møtte Jeffrey Epstein
headline_en: “I was 14 the first time I met Jeffrey Epstein”
lead_en: Victims of Jeffrey Epstein appeared publicly outside the U.S. Congress to reject claims that the case is a hoax and to demand greater transparency. They say they may help compile a list of prominent people Epstein allegedly procured girls for.
justification: The article is primarily a foreign news story about sexual abuse allegations, victims’ testimony, and U.S. political efforts to release documents, which does not fit the listed Norwegian election issue categories.

headline_no: Russlands ambassade om Høyre-uttalelser: – Vi blir stadig mer lei
headline_en: Russian embassy on Høyre’s statements: “We are getting increasingly tired of this”
lead_en: After a cyberattack on Høyre’s website, Erna Solberg suggested the timing was meant to create unrest during the election campaign. Russia’s embassy in Oslo rejects the accusation and says others could be behind the attack.
justification: The article is primarily about cyber interference, Russia, and security threats to Norwegian political processes, with a secondary governance angle related to the election campaign.

headline_no: Oljefondssjefen skal til USA – trues med innreisenekt
headline_en: The head of the sovereign wealth fund is going to the U.S. – threatened with denial of entry
lead_en: Nicolai Tangen is still planning a trip to New York, even as the Norwegian sovereign wealth fund faces political pressure from the U.S. after excluding Caterpillar over alleged human-rights risks linked to Israel’s conduct in conflict areas.
justification: The article primarily concerns the sovereign wealth fund’s exclusion of a company over alleged links to Israeli rights violations, placing it in the Gaza/sovereign fund category, with a secondary foreign-policy dimension.

headline_no: Er «manflu» ekte? Flere ting tyder på det.
headline_en: Is “man flu” real? Several things suggest it is.
lead_en: As cold season begins, the article asks whether men really experience respiratory infections differently from women. It cites experts and research suggesting biological sex differences in immune responses may make men more vulnerable to some infections.
justification: The article is primarily about health and biological differences in immune responses, placing it clearly in the health category.

headline_no: Sykefravaeret går nedover
headline_en: Sick leave is declining
lead_en: New figures from NAV and Statistics Norway show that sickness absence fell slightly in the second quarter, with declines in both self-certified and doctor-certified leave. The drop was especially notable for mental health-related absences, though the overall level remains high.
justification: The article is primarily about public health and workplace welfare through sickness absence trends and policy responses, with a secondary economic dimension related to labor force participation.

headline_no: Hjertespesialisten advarer: – Begynner allerede i tenårene
headline_en: Heart specialist warns: It already begins in the teenage years
lead_en: Cardiologist Thor Edvardsen says the processes that lead to heart disease often begin surprisingly early in life, sometimes already in adolescence. He highlights lifestyle prevention and warns that shortness of breath and high blood pressure are especially important warning signs.
justification: The article is primarily about heart disease prevention, risk factors, and medical warning signs, which clearly places it in the health category.

headline_no: Alt på én flate: – En trussel mot folkehelsen
headline_en: Everything on one level: “A threat to public health”
lead_en: The article argues that homes without stairs may reduce everyday physical activity and thereby harm public health. Using one man’s multistory house as an example, it explores whether daily movement built into housing can improve fitness and healthy ageing.
justification: The article is primarily about health, physical activity, and ageing, focusing on how housing design may affect everyday exercise and public health.

headline_no: Norge trenger gode venner, flere i arbeid og en mer nøysom stat
headline_en: Norway needs good friends, more people in work, and a more frugal state
lead_en: On the eve of the parliamentary election, Aftenposten argues that a changing world requires politicians who prioritize security, European cooperation, labour supply, fiscal restraint, and climate action.
justification: The article is primarily an election editorial about governing direction, coalition choices, and political leadership, while also giving strong weight to Norway’s security and European alignment.

headline_no: – Har avgitt troskapsed til Taliban
headline_en: “Has sworn an oath of allegiance to the Taliban”
lead_en: In the Oslo trial over the 2022 Pride terror attack, Arfan Qadeer Bhatti said he had sworn allegiance to the Taliban and that the oath still stands. Prosecutors argue he was the mastermind behind the attack, which he denies.
justification: The article is primarily about terrorism, extremism, and violent threat-related criminality in Norway, with a secondary foreign-policy/security dimension through Taliban, IS, and Afghanistan.

headline_no: Flertall for kutt i formuesskatten
headline_en: Majority supports cuts to the wealth tax
lead_en: An Aftenposten poll finds that more than half of respondents want the wealth tax reduced or abolished. The issue has become a major election topic after the Støre government increased the tax in 2022.
justification: The article is primarily about the wealth tax and public opinion on changing it, with a secondary governance angle because it discusses Labour’s search for a cross-bloc tax compromise.

headline_no: Han vokser seg sterk ved å spise grønt
headline_en: He is growing stronger by eating green
lead_en: Although climate has faded from the election campaign, MDG is gaining momentum and may emerge as an election winner. The article argues that the party is benefiting by attracting voters from other left-leaning and green parties.
justification: The article is primarily about election dynamics, voter movements, party strategy, and bloc balance, with climate as the substantive secondary issue tied to MDG’s appeal.

headline_no: Grepet blir kalt skamløst. Men Ap lover fortsatt rentekutt om de vinner valget.
headline_en: The move is being called shameless. But Labour still promises rate cuts if it wins the election.
lead_en: In the final stretch of the campaign, Labour defends its message that its economic policy will help bring interest rates down, even as opponents call the pledge misleading. The article centers on the clash between Ap and the right over rates, taxes, and fiscal responsibility.
justification: The article is primarily about interest rates, household borrowing costs, and competing economic strategies, with a secondary governance angle because it is framed as an election-campaign dispute over credibility and competence.

headline_no: Har søkt seg til prestisjetunge internasjonale forretningsskoler
headline_en: Has applied to prestigious international business schools
lead_en: While campaigning hard for re-election, Centre Party politician Emilie Enger Mehl has also been making plans for possible full-time study abroad. The article focuses on uncertainty around her political future and role in the party after the election.
justification: The article is primarily about political career prospects, party leadership, and parliamentary positioning rather than any specific policy issue, so it fits governance and leadership.

headline_no: Denne skolen var en ikonisk Høyrebastion. Nå er Frp suverent størst.
headline_en: This school was an iconic Conservative stronghold. Now the Progress Party is by far the largest.
lead_en: The article examines Oslo school election results, where Frp has overtaken Høyre at a flagship conservative school while SV has surpassed Ap in eastern Oslo and at elite central schools. It is a snapshot of shifting youth support and party competition ahead of the election.
justification: The article is primarily about electoral competition, polling dynamics, and party strength among student voters, and it casts Labour as losing ground to SV in key Oslo school environments.

headline_no: Rødt er lei «pekeleken» – vil legge ned Etikkrådet
headline_en: Rødt is tired of the “blame game” – wants to abolish the Ethics Council
lead_en: After revelations about the sovereign wealth fund’s investments in companies linked to Israel’s war in Gaza, Rødt proposes a major overhaul of the fund’s ethical oversight. The party wants a new body under parliament and demands divestment from Israeli companies.
justification: The article is primarily about Gaza-related demands to reform the sovereign wealth fund’s ethical rules and divest from Israeli-linked companies, with a secondary governance dimension about who should control oversight.

headline_no: Ivrig jakt på ytringer gir britene trøbbel
headline_en: Eager policing of speech is causing trouble for the British
lead_en: A commentary argues that British police are going too far in pursuing controversial speech online, using the case of Irish comedian Graham Linehan and other examples involving trans issues and pro-Palestinian activism. It says this creates legal and political problems in the UK.
justification: The article is primarily a commentary on free speech, policing, and legal boundaries in the UK, which does not fit the main election issue categories provided.

headline_no: Da studentene ville ut, fikk de en faktura på 357.570 kroner
headline_en: When the students wanted out, they received an invoice for 357,570 kroner
lead_en: Four students tried to end their rental contract after nine months, but the landlord says they are bound for much longer and has demanded more than 357,000 kroner. The case highlights disputes over fixed-term leases and consumer concerns in the rental market.
justification: The article is primarily about housing and consumer welfare in the rental market, which fits public services/welfare better than party-political issue categories, with a secondary cost-of-living angle due to the large financial burden.

headline_no: Kommunen vil kaste ham ut av Frognerkilen
headline_en: The municipality wants to evict him from Frognerkilen
lead_en: Oslo authorities want to remove dozens of boats they say are illegally moored in Frognerkilen, including some that people live in. The dispute could become an important test case for coastal municipalities across Norway.
justification: The article is primarily about housing and welfare concerns for people living on boats, with a secondary environmental angle related to sunken boats, pollution, and safety risks.

headline_no: Vil ha Frogner på Furuset
headline_en: Wants Frogner on Furuset
lead_en: A debate over redevelopment at Furuset has sparked calls for more classical, human-scale architecture, with support from MDG. The article contrasts demands for aesthetic quality with pressure to maximize housing construction.
justification: The article is primarily about urban development and housing quality in Oslo, which fits welfare/public services better than the other categories, with a secondary environmental angle through greener and more human-scaled city planning.

headline_no: 390.000 norske kvinner har ikke tatt HPV-test
headline_en: 390,000 Norwegian women have not taken an HPV test
lead_en: Norwegian health authorities are concerned that 390,000 women have still not taken the HPV test used in the cervical screening program, even after reminders. The test can detect cancer risk and disease earlier than older smear-based screening.
justification: The article is primarily about public health screening, cancer prevention, and participation in Norway’s cervical screening program, which clearly fits the health category.

headline_no: Hvorfor skyter det amerikanske militaeret på en båt i Karibia?
headline_en: Why is the U.S. military firing on a boat in the Caribbean?
lead_en: After Donald Trump said U.S. forces struck a boat carrying alleged Venezuelan “narco-terrorists,” the article examines whether the gang Tren de Aragua is really a serious threat to the United States. Experts cited in the piece argue that the danger is being overstated.
justification: The article is primarily about U.S. military action, international security, and foreign-policy framing around Venezuela, even though it also touches on crime and migration.

headline_no: Den israelske haeren hevder å kontrollere 40 prosent av Gaza by
headline_en: The Israeli army claims to control 40 percent of Gaza City
lead_en: Israel says its ground forces now control about 40 percent of Gaza City and are advancing toward full control of the city. The article also notes mass displacement, rising death tolls, and Norway’s condemnation of the plan.
justification: The article is primarily about the Gaza war and Norway’s response to Israel’s military offensive and forced displacement of Palestinians, with a secondary broader security and foreign-policy dimension.

headline_no: Folketeatret: – Spektakulaer forestilling
headline_en: Folketeatret: “A spectacular performance”
lead_en: The article reports that Donald Trump is restoring the name “War Department” alongside “Department of Defense,” framing it as part of a broader shift toward a more openly militarized posture in the United States.
justification: The article is primarily about U.S. defence policy, military posture, and international security symbolism, which fits the security/foreign policy category.

headline_no: Flere kan få rett til erstatning når de skader seg på jobb
headline_en: More people may gain the right to compensation when they are injured at work
lead_en: The government wants to change compensation rules so workers injured in ordinary but risky job situations can more easily qualify for occupational injury compensation. The proposal is especially relevant for health care, construction, and emergency-service occupations.
justification: The article is primarily about worker welfare and compensation rights in everyday job injuries, with a secondary governance aspect because it centers on a government reform proposal led by Labour.

headline_no: Hvor ofte må man bytte ut hygieniske artikler?
headline_en: How often should you replace hygiene items?
lead_en: A hygiene expert explains how often everyday items such as toothbrushes, kitchen cloths, razors, and mascara should be cleaned or replaced. The article warns that poor routines can allow bacteria, fungi, and other microbes to spread.
justification: The article is primarily about hygiene, infection prevention, and health risks in everyday life, which clearly places it in the health category.

headline_no: Se opp for søndagens blodmåne
headline_en: Watch out for Sunday’s blood moon
lead_en: A total lunar eclipse will be visible across most of Norway on Sunday evening, giving people a chance to see a dramatic red “blood moon.” Experts say the event may be especially memorable because the Moon will be low on the horizon.
justification: The article is primarily a science and astronomy piece about a lunar eclipse visible in Norway, which does not fit the main political issue categories and is therefore coded as other.

headline_no: Huset fra 1937 er ikke til å kjenne igjen
headline_en: The house from 1937 is unrecognizable
lead_en: A couple in Lillehammer fully renovated and expanded a 1937 house, turning it into a colorful and highly personalized home. The piece focuses on interior design choices, craftsmanship, and inspiration drawn from nature.
justification: The article is a lifestyle and home-interior feature about renovation and design, not a political or policy issue covered by the main coding categories.

headline_no: Netflix-trailer om Märtha Louise og Durek Verrett klar
headline_en: Netflix trailer for Märtha Louise and Durek Verrett released
lead_en: Netflix has released the trailer for a documentary about Märtha Louise and Durek Verrett, prompting criticism that it violates the couple’s 2022 agreement with the royal household. Commentators say the production uses royal branding in a clearly commercial context.
justification: The article is primarily about the royal family, media, and a commercial documentary project, which does not fit the main political issue categories and is therefore coded as other.

headline_no: Studie: Mobil gjør at vi blunker sjeldnere
headline_en: Study: Mobile phones make us blink less often
lead_en: A new study finds that even one hour a day on a smartphone can strain the eyes, especially when scrolling through social media. Researchers say reduced blinking and changing screen stimuli may contribute to digital eye strain.
justification: The article is primarily about eye health, screen use, and the health effects of smartphone behavior, so it fits the health category.

headline_no: Ap på vei inn i et «tåkete og gråsonet landskap»
headline_en: Labour is heading into a “foggy and grey-zone landscape”
lead_en: The article argues that Labour is moving into a politically murky period in which it may have to rely on a broad and difficult set of support parties to stay in power. Jens Stoltenberg’s contrast between economics and politics is used to frame the challenges ahead.
justification: The piece is mainly about Labour’s governing prospects, coalition constraints, and political leadership challenges rather than a specific policy issue.

headline_no: Elsai Mousavi (16) kan bli Norges yngste lege
headline_en: Elsai Mousavi (16) could become Norway’s youngest doctor
lead_en: Elsai Mousavi, 16, has started medical school in Oslo after finishing upper secondary school early, following in the footsteps of her older sister. The article describes her academic path, family background, and efforts to balance studies with friends and leisure.
justification: The article is primarily about entry into medical education and the future health profession, with a secondary human-interest angle rather than party politics.

headline_no: Kampensiktet sendte truende melding til barnevernsansatt
headline_en: Kampen murder suspect sent threatening message to child welfare employee
lead_en: The 18-year-old charged in the Kampen killing had previously sent a threatening message to a child welfare employee, according to NRK and confirmed by police. The article also reports new details in the murder investigation and the terror-related charge.
justification: The article centers on violent crime and threats linked to a child welfare setting, fitting best under crime, with a secondary public-services dimension because the victim and threatened person worked in care institutions.

headline_no: Brakmåling for Ap, men fortsatt avhengig av alle de rødgrønne
headline_en: Huge poll for Labour, but still dependent on all the red-green parties
lead_en: A final pre-election poll shows Labour surging to 29.5 percent and leading the other parties by a wide margin. Even so, Jonas Gahr Støre would still need support from all four other red-green parties to secure a majority for a new Labour government.
justification: The article is primarily about polling, electoral momentum, and government formation, and it frames Labour’s position as strongly improved ahead of the election.

headline_no: 1,9 millioner velgere har forhåndsstemt
headline_en: 1.9 million voters have cast advance ballots
lead_en: Nearly 1.9 million Norwegians had voted before election day, equal to 47.1 percent of eligible voters. The article reports record-high advance voting and large geographical differences, with Oslo among the districts with the highest early turnout.
justification: The article is about election administration and voter turnout ahead of the parliamentary election, which fits governance and the electoral process rather than a substantive policy issue.

headline_no: Pr-topp mener SV stigmatiserer milliardaerer
headline_en: PR executive says SV is stigmatizing billionaires
lead_en: PR adviser Margrethe Geelmuyden accuses SV of using dehumanizing rhetoric against billionaires after comments that Norway does not need billionaires. SV’s Alexander Papas replies that the issue is redistribution of wealth and power through tougher taxation.
justification: The article is primarily about the wealth-tax and redistribution debate, framed through a dispute over rhetoric toward billionaires, with only a secondary party-political conflict dimension.

headline_no: Kina gir full gass for grønn omstilling
headline_en: China is going full throttle on the green transition
lead_en: The article argues that China is becoming the world’s leading force in the real green transition, while the United States under Donald Trump is clinging to fossil energy. It highlights China’s massive investments in renewables, electric transport, and energy infrastructure.
justification: The article is primarily about climate and energy transition policy, especially China’s expansion of renewables and clean technology, with a secondary geopolitical competition angle.

headline_no: Underjordiske klasserom tryggest for ukrainske barna
headline_en: Underground classrooms are safest for Ukrainian children
lead_en: As the school year begins in Ukraine, some schools are teaching in basements to protect children from Russian attacks while allowing them to meet in person. The article follows one school in the Sumy region that has moved classes underground for safety.
justification: The article is primarily about schooling and children’s access to education as a public service under wartime conditions, with a secondary security context because the underground classrooms are a response to Russian attacks.

headline_no: USA stopper støtte til forsvaret av de baltiske landene
headline_en: The U.S. is stopping support for the defence of the Baltic states
lead_en: The article reports that the Trump administration may end U.S. funding for defence and security programs in the Baltic states next year. It also outlines Norway’s current military contributions in the region and the broader European push to assume more responsibility.
justification: The article is primarily about defence, NATO’s eastern flank, and U.S.-European security burden-sharing, with only a secondary governance element through the Norwegian minister’s response.

headline_no: Inga Strümke: Ikke overraskende har mange falt for fristelsen til å be kunstig intelligens om hjelp til å velge politisk parti.
headline_en: Inga Strümke: It is not surprising that many have been tempted to ask artificial intelligence for help choosing a political party.
lead_en: Many voters have turned to AI chatbots for help choosing a party before the election. The author argues that this is tempting, but misleading, because language models are not designed to uncover real-world political truths.
justification: The article is primarily about election-related voter guidance and how people use AI to choose parties, which fits governance and political decision-making rather than a substantive policy issue.

headline_no: Mann siktet for drap etter voldshendelse i Oslo
headline_en: Man charged with murder after violent incident in Oslo
lead_en: Police launched a murder investigation after finding a man in his 30s dead in an apartment on Torshov in Oslo. Several people have been arrested, and police are searching for additional named suspects.
justification: The article is primarily a local crime report about a homicide investigation in Oslo, which does not center on election-related policy and therefore fits Other.

headline_no: Trump truer Chicago med apokalyptisk makt
headline_en: Trump threatens Chicago with apocalyptic force
lead_en: Donald Trump repeated promises to send the National Guard to Chicago and shared a meme depicting a wartime-style assault on the city. Illinois Governor JB Pritzker condemned the post as authoritarian and dangerous.
justification: The article centers on security-related use of state force and Trump’s threats involving the National Guard, with a secondary governance dimension about executive power.

headline_no: Benjamin (18) stemte for første gang – på ingen
headline_en: Benjamin (18) voted for the first time – for no one
lead_en: First-time voter Benjamin Reitan cast a blank ballot in Kristiansund, saying no party had earned his support. The article examines the small but growing number of Norwegian voters who choose to vote blank.
justification: The article is about voter behavior, party disaffection, and electoral participation rather than a substantive policy issue, so it fits governance and politics.

headline_no: Fortsatt folkets favoritt
headline_en: Still the people’s favorite
lead_en: One day before election day, a new poll shows Jonas Gahr Støre remains the most popular choice for prime minister. He leads clearly over both Erna Solberg and Sylvi Listhaug.
justification: The article is a poll-driven horse-race story about prime minister preferences and political leadership, which fits governance, and it frames Labour’s leader positively by emphasizing Støre’s strong lead.

headline_no: Er det viktig om Listhaug eller Solberg blir statsminister?
headline_en: Does it matter whether Listhaug or Solberg becomes prime minister?
lead_en: A majority of centre-right voters say it matters who becomes prime minister. The article focuses on the dispute between Erna Solberg and Sylvi Listhaug over leadership on the right ahead of the election.
justification: The article is about leadership, coalition dynamics, and voter preferences over who should lead a centre-right government, which fits governance rather than a policy issue.

headline_no: Vi vet hva du mest sannsynlig stemmer
headline_en: We know what you are most likely to vote for
lead_en: On election day, Aftenposten maps the typical voter profile for each Norwegian party based on gender, age, income, education, and geography. The article explains which issues and social characteristics are most associated with support for each party.
justification: The article is an election analysis of party support, voter demographics, and campaign positioning rather than a specific policy issue, so it fits governance and electoral politics.

headline_no: Norge skal bruke over 1600 milliarder. Knapt diskutert i valgkampen.
headline_en: Norway will spend more than 1,600 billion. It has barely been discussed in the election campaign.
lead_en: Norway plans to spend NOK 1,624 billion on defence over the next 12 years, yet security policy has barely featured in the election campaign. Researchers say broad party agreement and media priorities help explain the lack of debate.
justification: The article is primarily about defence, security policy, and Norway’s military spending in response to the international situation, with a secondary governance angle about campaign priorities and party competition.

headline_no: Hvor kommer barnas fordommer fra?
headline_en: Where do children’s prejudices come from?
lead_en: A TV 2 series about children from Oslo’s east and west shows how deeply social and cultural divisions shape prejudice. The commentary argues that these divisions are rooted in segregation, family attitudes, and the housing market.
justification: The article is primarily about social divisions, integration, and prejudice in ethnically and socially segregated parts of Oslo, which best fits immigration/integration.

headline_no: Ukrainske barn blir Putins soldater
headline_en: Ukrainian children are becoming Putin’s soldiers
lead_en: The article describes how children from Russian-occupied parts of Ukraine are being deported, indoctrinated, and trained for military service by Russian authorities. It also notes Norwegian concern, including Jonas Gahr Støre’s comments after meeting rescued Ukrainian children in Kyiv.
justification: The article is primarily about war, occupation, militarization, and the Russia-Ukraine conflict, which clearly places it in security/foreign policy.

headline_no: Australsk satire gjør narr av Norge
headline_en: Australian satire mocks Norway
lead_en: A popular Australian satire video portrays Norway as self-righteous and hostile to the climate because of its oil and gas wealth. The article discusses whether the criticism hits a nerve in Norway’s self-image and climate debate.
justification: The article is primarily about criticism of Norway’s oil, gas, and climate hypocrisy, which places it in climate/environment and oil policy.

headline_no: Professor: Diplomatbarn har svakere rettslig beskyttelse
headline_en: Professor: Diplomats’ children have weaker legal protection
lead_en: The article examines how diplomatic immunity can leave diplomats’ children less protected when abuse or danger occurs inside the home. Researchers argue that these children may have weaker legal safeguards than other children because authorities cannot intervene in the usual way.
justification: The article is primarily about child protection and limits on welfare-state intervention in vulnerable families, with a secondary international/diplomatic dimension.

headline_no: Norske turistmagneter: Seks mil med stier legges ned
headline_en: Norwegian tourist magnets: Sixty kilometers of trails are being closed down
lead_en: The Norwegian Trekking Association is removing trail markings and limiting access in Dovrefjell and Rondane to protect threatened wild reindeer. Critics argue the measures come too late and that tourism itself has helped create the problem.
justification: The article is primarily about environmental protection and conservation measures to protect wild reindeer habitats from tourism pressure, which fits climate/environment.

headline_no: De ser helt ulikt på tid: – Noen ting kan man aldri forandre
headline_en: They see time completely differently: “Some things you can never change”
lead_en: The article follows a couple with opposite attitudes to punctuality and explores how “time optimists” and “time realists” experience time differently. A psychologist explains how these traits can create stress and conflict, but also be adjusted somewhat.
justification: This is a lifestyle and psychology feature about punctuality and personality rather than an article about electoral politics or one of the defined policy domains.

headline_no: Kunstig uintelligent
headline_en: Artificially unintelligent
lead_en: The commentary argues that employees who use AI at work may be judged as less competent, especially women and older workers. It highlights research suggesting that social stigma is slowing workplace adoption of AI tools.
justification: This is primarily a workplace and technology commentary about AI adoption and stigma, which does not fit the main election issue categories, though it has a secondary economic/workplace dimension.

headline_no: Nå haster det med press på Russland
headline_en: Now there is urgency to increase pressure on Russia
lead_en: Russia launched a record wave of drones and missiles at Ukraine, showing again that Putin has no real interest in peace. The editorial argues that the West must respond with more military aid to Ukraine and much tougher sanctions on Russia.
justification: The article is primarily about the war in Ukraine, Western sanctions, military aid, and Europe’s and the U.S.’s security response toward Russia.

headline_no: Drømmen om det analoge samfunn
headline_en: The dream of the analogue society
lead_en: A Japanese city wants to introduce a daily limit on smartphone use, even for adults, as part of a broader reaction against digital overuse. The columnist uses the case to reflect on screen addiction, symbolic regulation, and similar debates in Norway and other countries.
justification: The article is mainly a social commentary on smartphone use and digital dependency, which does not fit the main election issue categories.

headline_no: Sjokk og sorg
headline_en: Shock and sorrow
lead_en: Four young women aged 18 and 19 died in a house fire in Hamar, one of Norway’s deadliest fires in recent years. The article reports on the emergency response, the local community’s grief, and the ongoing investigation into the cause.
justification: The article is primarily a news report about a fatal house fire and public mourning, not an election-policy issue, though it secondarily touches on crisis response and public support services.

headline_no: Hun og hennes dommerkolleger kan avgjøre flere av Trumps hjertesaker
headline_en: She and her fellow justices could decide several of Trump’s signature issues
lead_en: Supreme Court Justice Amy Coney Barrett says judges can be unpredictable as the court heads into major cases tied to Donald Trump’s agenda. The article highlights disputes over presidential power, tariffs, citizenship, and other issues likely to come before the U.S. Supreme Court.
justification: The article mainly concerns U.S. power and international trade policy with direct implications for Norway through tariffs, while secondarily touching on institutional power and judicial oversight.

headline_no: Kommunen vil kutte SFO i skoleferiene
headline_en: The municipality wants to cut after-school care during school holidays
lead_en: Lørenskog municipality is considering cutting SFO provision on school-free days and reducing opening hours to save money. Parents warn that the proposal would make everyday life much harder and hurt children, especially more vulnerable families.
justification: The article is primarily about cuts to childcare and school-related public services, with a secondary link to municipal budget pressures.

headline_no: Eviny-sjefen: Forstår at folk reagerer
headline_en: Eviny chief: I understand why people are reacting
lead_en: Eviny’s CEO says the power industry must rebuild public trust after the electricity crisis and criticism of executive pay. The article also discusses power taxation, investment in energy production, and debate over electricity prices and the government’s Norgespris policy.
justification: The article mainly focuses on electricity prices, the power sector, and affordability-related energy politics, with a secondary emphasis on taxation of power companies.

headline_no: Disse sakene ble «glemt» i valgkampen
headline_en: These issues were “forgotten” in the election campaign
lead_en: Economists argue that the campaign has focused too narrowly on the wealth tax while neglecting broader debates on inheritance tax, dividend tax, productivity, labour supply, and falling birth rates. The piece critiques the lack of a more comprehensive discussion of Norway’s long-term economic challenges.
justification: The article is primarily about tax policy debates in the election campaign, especially wealth, inheritance, and dividend taxation, with a secondary focus on broader economic performance and productivity.

headline_no: Dette var viktigst blant de som forhåndsstemte
headline_en: This mattered most to those who voted early
lead_en: A survey of early voters finds that social inequality is the single most important issue for the largest share of voters, ahead of taxes and the economy. Researchers say the election is increasingly being shaped by pocketbook concerns and a renewed left-right economic divide.
justification: The article is primarily about voter priorities and election dynamics based on polling of early voters, with a secondary emphasis on economic concerns.

headline_no: Dette skjer nå
headline_en: This is what happens now
lead_en: Regardless of who won the election, Jonas Gahr Støre remains prime minister for about another month while Norway follows the formal process of government formation. The article explains likely minority-government scenarios, coalition talks, and the timetable for the new parliament and the 2026 budget.
justification: The article is primarily about government formation, coalition possibilities, parliamentary procedure, and post-election leadership dynamics.

headline_no: Politiets nye grep mot narkotika: Fjerner muligheten til å ta ut kontanter
headline_en: The police’s new move against drugs: removing the ability to withdraw cash
lead_en: Police and Oslo municipality have removed several ATMs in central Oslo after linking them to drug dealing, robberies, and other crime. The article describes the strategy, local reactions, and the legal dispute over whether the municipality has the authority to shut the machines down.
justification: The article is primarily about drug-related crime and policing in Oslo, with a secondary governance angle concerning municipal authority and legal appeals.

headline_no: Ikke svensk nok? Nå hagler kritikken.
headline_en: Not Swedish enough? Now the criticism is pouring in.
lead_en: A Sweden Democrats politician is under heavy criticism after describing two politicians debating Sweden’s cultural canon as “a Kurd and a Persian.” The controversy has sparked a broader debate about ethnicity, belonging, and who counts as fully Swedish.
justification: The article is primarily about identity, ethnicity, and integration in Swedish politics, with a secondary focus on tensions within the governing right-wing bloc.

headline_no: Spania innfører våpenboikott mot Israel umiddelbart
headline_en: Spain introduces an immediate arms boycott against Israel
lead_en: Spain is immediately imposing an arms boycott and other sanctions against Israel, saying the measures are meant to help stop what Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez calls the genocide in Gaza. The article also describes Israel’s strong backlash and Spain’s broader steps, including port, airspace, and settlement-goods restrictions.
justification: The article is primarily about the Gaza war and state action against Israel, with a secondary foreign-policy and sanctions dimension.

headline_no: De villeste trafikantene i Oslo er folk på elsparkesykkel
headline_en: The wildest road users in Oslo are people on e-scooters
lead_en: The columnist argues that e-scooter riders and cyclists in Oslo frequently ignore red lights because enforcement is weak and consequences are rare. He suggests that stronger policing, fines, and better cycling infrastructure would reduce risky behaviour.
justification: The article is primarily an urban traffic and public-order commentary about road behaviour and enforcement, which does not fit the main election issue categories.

headline_no: Gule blader fra tidlig august: – Overlevelsesstrategi
headline_en: Yellow leaves from early August: a survival strategy
lead_en: Many trees appeared to turn yellow unusually early, but a forestry researcher says this is mainly a response to a dry, warm summer. Early leaf loss helps deciduous trees conserve water and energy under drought stress.
justification: The article is primarily about environmental conditions and how dry, warm weather affects trees, making climate/environment the best fit.

headline_no: Trenddrikk tømmer japanske tegårder
headline_en: Trend drink is emptying Japanese tea farms
lead_en: Global demand for matcha has surged as the drink spreads through social media and Western cafés, leaving Japanese producers struggling to keep up. The article describes supply shortages, rising prices, and structural pressures on Japan’s tea industry.
justification: The article is primarily about a consumer and cultural trend affecting Japan’s tea industry, with a secondary economic angle related to supply, prices, and trade.

headline_no: 300 koreanere arrestert på Hyundai-fabrikk i USA
headline_en: 300 Koreans arrested at Hyundai factory in the US
lead_en: U.S. immigration authorities carried out a massive raid at a Hyundai-LG battery plant in Georgia and arrested 475 people, including 300 South Koreans. The article focuses on immigration enforcement, alleged unauthorized employment, and the political context of tougher U.S. crackdowns.
justification: The article is primarily about immigration enforcement and undocumented labor, with a secondary economic dimension tied to factory construction and labor practices.

headline_no: Ny behandling for ryggsmerter: – Et gjennombrudd innen forskning
headline_en: New treatment for back pain: a breakthrough in research
lead_en: Studies on chronic back pain found that patients receiving cognitive functional therapy improved far more than those getting usual care, with many close to recovery even after three years. Norwegian experts describe the findings as a major research breakthrough with potential to change treatment practice.
justification: The article is primarily about healthcare treatment and medical research on chronic back pain, making health the clear main issue.

headline_no: – Det skremmer meg når jeg ser bamser i barnevogner og senger
headline_en: It frightens me when I see teddy bears in prams and beds
lead_en: A Norwegian mother is warning parents about microplastics in stuffed toys, arguing that children should not keep synthetic plush toys close to their faces. The article also notes that experts see plastic exposure as a real concern, but say research is still lacking on how important teddy bears are as a source.
justification: The article is primarily about possible health risks from microplastic exposure in children, with a secondary environmental angle related to plastic pollution.

headline_no: Støre inviterer til skattejakt. Har selv bundet seg fast.
headline_en: Støre invites a tax hunt. Has tied himself down.
lead_en: After Labour’s election victory, Jonas Gahr Støre again invited parties to join a tax commission to review Norway’s tax system. Unlike earlier this year, opposition parties are now more open to a broad tax settlement.
justification: The article is primarily about tax policy and the need for a broad tax reform, while criticizing Labour for binding itself with campaign promises that may hinder such a deal.

headline_no: Kjetil B. Alstadheim: Disse tre kan overta etter Solberg. Én av dem peker seg ut.
headline_en: Kjetil B. Alstadheim: These three could take over after Solberg. One stands out.
lead_en: After Høyre’s poor election result, attention is turning to who might succeed Erna Solberg as party leader. Ine Eriksen Søreide appears to be the strongest candidate, though Henrik Asheim and Peter C. Frølich are also discussed.
justification: The article is mainly about party leadership, succession, and strategic renewal in Høyre, which makes governance and political leadership the dominant issue.

headline_no: Jakten på etterfølgeren er i gang
headline_en: The search for a successor is underway
lead_en: After Erna Solberg announced she will not seek re-election, pressure is mounting for her to step down quickly. The article also examines how wealthy donors on the right backed the election campaign but still failed to unseat Jonas Gahr Støre.
justification: The article primarily concerns leadership change, election fallout, and the future direction of Høyre, with a secondary emphasis on the wealth-tax debate and donor influence.

headline_no: «Verdikonservativ blokk» kan gi Støre kraftig hodebry
headline_en: “A values-conservative bloc” could give Støre a major headache
lead_en: Although Labour won the election, it lacks a parliamentary majority and may face defeats in the Storting. The article highlights how the Centre Party could cooperate with parties on the right in selected issues, complicating governance for Støre.
justification: The article is mainly about parliamentary arithmetic, coalition dynamics, and the difficulties Labour may face governing without a majority, with tax policy as a secondary issue.

headline_no: En helt utrolig revansj for Støre
headline_en: A truly incredible comeback for Støre
lead_en: Jonas Gahr Støre remains prime minister after Labour both increased its support and kept power. The piece argues that Sylvi Listhaug’s polarizing role and Jens Stoltenberg’s return as finance minister were key reasons for Labour’s revival.
justification: The article primarily analyzes Labour’s electoral comeback, leadership, and governing position after the election, while secondarily noting economic conditions that helped Støre.

headline_no: – Skatteløftet vårt ligger fast, selvfølgelig
headline_en: “Our tax pledge still stands, of course”
lead_en: Jonas Gahr Støre says Labour will keep its promise not to raise the overall tax level, even as he seeks talks with other parties on how to govern. He also confirmed that Jens Stoltenberg will continue as finance minister and that Labour will pursue a broader tax settlement.
justification: The article is primarily about Labour’s tax pledge and plans for a broader tax agreement, with governance and parliamentary cooperation as a secondary theme.

headline_no: – Veldig motivert for å fortsette
headline_en: “Very motivated to continue”
lead_en: Jens Stoltenberg says he is very motivated to remain finance minister after Labour’s election win. The article also notes that he wants renewed talks on a tax settlement, though he made a small verbal slip by calling himself prime minister.
justification: The article is mainly about Stoltenberg’s role in the government and Labour’s post-election leadership continuity, with tax policy as a secondary issue.

headline_no: Høyre-topper vil at hun går av
headline_en: Høyre figures want her to step down
lead_en: After Høyre’s worst election since 2005, senior party figures are signaling that Erna Solberg should step aside as leader. The article focuses on the growing internal pressure, possible successors, and the party’s coming renewal process.
justification: The article is primarily about party leadership, succession, and internal evaluation in Høyre after an election defeat, which fits governance and political leadership.

headline_no: Lang liste med krav fra «tuttifrutti»-gjengen
headline_en: Long list of demands from the “tuttifrutti” gang
lead_en: Labour can continue in office, but Jonas Gahr Støre needs support from MDG, SV, Rødt and the Centre Party to secure parliamentary majorities. The article outlines the many policy demands these parties are already making in exchange for backing the government.
justification: The article is primarily about coalition bargaining and the demands smaller left-wing parties are making on Labour to govern, with Gaza-related sovereign wealth fund divestment as a secondary issue.

headline_no: Brakvalg for Nordkalottfolket
headline_en: Landslide result for Nordkalottfolket
lead_en: The Sami parliamentary election may produce a new majority that supports wind power and the electrification of the Melkøya gas facility. Labour holds the balance of power and could help install Nordkalottfolket in leadership.
justification: The article is primarily about energy, wind power, electrification, and related environmental-land use conflicts, with a secondary focus on coalition dynamics in the Sami Parliament.

headline_no: Tøff kamp om plassene på Stortinget
headline_en: Tough battle for seats in the Storting
lead_en: The article reviews which prominent politicians won or lost seats after the election, as the new parliament takes shape. It highlights shifts in party representation, the age and gender profile of the Storting, and several notable individual outcomes.
justification: The article is primarily about parliamentary composition, election outcomes, and which politicians enter or leave the Storting, making governance and political representation the main issue.

headline_no: Fikk kun 765 stemmer – er inne på Stortinget
headline_en: Got only 765 votes – is in the Storting
lead_en: Despite receiving only 765 votes in Finnmark, MDG’s Siren Jensen won a seat in parliament because the party passed the national threshold for leveling seats. The article explains how Norway’s electoral system can produce such outcomes and also notes the broader election debate over the wealth tax.
justification: The article is primarily about electoral representation and how Norway’s seat-allocation system works, with a secondary focus on the wealth-tax debate in the election campaign.

headline_no: Deler fire hjerter etter branntragedien på Hamar
headline_en: Sharing four hearts after the fire tragedy in Hamar
lead_en: Four young women aged 18–19 died in a house fire in Hamar, prompting widespread mourning in the local community. The article focuses on the tragedy, the ongoing investigation, and the support shown by residents and local authorities.
justification: The article is about a local fire tragedy, mourning, and the emergency response, which does not fit the main election-related issue categories.

headline_no: Stopper salget av én champagne etter juks
headline_en: Halts the sale of one champagne after fraud
lead_en: Vinmonopolet has stopped selling one champagne after the French producer behind it was convicted of selling fake champagne. The bottle sold in Norway has been sent for laboratory analysis to verify whether it is genuine.
justification: The article is about consumer fraud and wine sales controls, which does not fit the main political issue categories.
